## It Is Here!!!
---
|
---
It is FINALLY here! I have been very busy signing all the pre-orders for my newest book entitled '[DIVIDED: BREAKING FREE FROM THE DOUBLE LIFE](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=27294135.14001.1.7bedcddebe4b4f696b9279f55785b047).' **Pastor Paula White-Cain** wrote an _incredible_ Foreword and **Rabbi Jonathan Cahn** gave an _amazing_ Endorsement. In this, I open up about _my own personal struggles_ as a follower of Jesus and a Pastor. I share things that I've never shared publicly in hopes that it will speak to people who might be ashamed with the different struggles they have even as Christians and how to have victory. There are two of us in all of us but we're not destined to live **DIVIDED** forever. You can visit: [https://clr.cm/qPKQ ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=27294135.14002.1.69382d792567c6e761c353e140ea5673)to get your SIGNED copy and start reading immediately with the **FREE e-book** that's included. You can also get a copy on [Amazon](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=27294135.14003.1.4ab74926bc78f0b13573eac50099464f), [Barnes & Noble](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=27294135.14004.1.2f29e5ae8100ca547e498eb5e69c4cf0), and all other book retailers as well. I believe this book will help many people overcome the double life that far too many of us live. Also, join me on NEWSMAX tonight at 8:45pm EST with David Harris Jr. Thank you -Pastor Jackson Lahmeyer
---
| | [ Click Here To Read "DIVIDED" Now ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=27294135.14005.1.ab0ddac2bf29d20e2a4e4a603eac18d9)
---
| [ ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=27294135.14006.1.574cae709ebcc7e44e1f1447f3bdc641)
---
Every one of us lives with tension - the tug-of-war between the two of us living in all of us. In DIVIDED, Pastor Jackson Lahmeyer gets real, raw, and deeply relatable as he opens up about his own struggles with pride, anger, and failure while chasing God's purpose. Through humor, honesty, and hard-learned wisdom, Lahmeyer invites readers to drop the mask, confront the "dark me" that keeps surfacing, and experience the transforming power of grace. This is not a polished preacher's pep talk-it's a gut-level, heart-to-heart conversation about what it means to follow Jesus when life gets messy. Drawing from the lives of biblical characters like Peter, Jacob and Paul, Lahmeyer shows that even the greatest heroes of faith had moments of weakness, doubt, and defeat. He unpacks the difference between remorse and repentance, explores how belief shapes behavior, and reveals how identity-not effort-is the key to lasting transformation. With vivid stories, relatable honesty, and the truth of Scripture, he reminds us that God isn't looking for the perfect version of you-He's after the real you. Because the moment you get real with God is the moment He starts making you new. If you've ever felt like you're living two lives-the public you that looks strong and the private you that's barely hanging on-this book is for you. DIVIDED will help you see that failure isn't final, temptation isn't sin, and your past doesn't disqualify your purpose. You'll laugh, you'll be challenged, and you'll find hope in the God who meets you where you are but never leaves you there. Honest, gritty, and full of grace, this book will inspire you to feed the right wolf, renew your mind, and finally live as the "you" God designed you to be.
---
---
|
---

Community Release: January 6, 2026
Colorado's CD3 GOP Primary Voters Prefer Hope Scheppelman Over Incumbent Jeff Hurd in Key Polling Metrics:

Grand Junction & Pueblo, CO: Today, the Hope Scheppelman for Congress campaign publicly released the attached Internal Polling Memo showing the promising results from polling done last month.

FROM THE POLLSTER: "I've polled quite a few races like this in the '26 cycle and, despite the 43% 'not sure' favorability rating, Hope performs the best against the incumbent of any of the districts. This is a huge opportunity to grow an even bigger lead." Mark Mitchell, Head Pollster, Rasmussen Reports.

While narrowly trailing incumbent Jeff Hurd in the initial head-to-head matchup, as would be expected, the race swings dramatically in Scheppelman's favor once voters know basic information about both candidates.
"Our polling confirms everyone's instincts about Hurd's freshman blundering with his radical Democrat buddies when he should be supporting President Trump and us and our district's commonsense America First values," said Hope Scheppelman
"I'm listening closely to voters, and they are done with Hurd's left-wing insider antics and are more than willing to give me the chance to fight for our hard-working and law-abiding citizens in Congress," continued Scheppelman.
With Colorado's Republican Party Caucus Nomination process beginning in 2 months, this polling data comes at the best time for undecided voters to begin to understand the two candidates better.
"Regular Republicans and common-sense Democrats and Unaffiliated voters should be encouraged by these results, too, because they show we can do much better than sell-out insider politicians like Jeff Hurd," concluded Scheppelman.
For more information and to contact Hope, please go to her website to donate, comment, or volunteer today: [www.hopeforcolorado.com](https://a5qvizhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001xn9EBZZoASPZh8rm5CoXmAh8tMFjJSHaWOnpJHC5-wZ19b8bCFx3WeYoMLyAOD5qSGKXbTYAv4OCWUA69j3DCvJO7QWMfW6eZof9652XWTznSU8e_ZnWE1bZ7Mr4f4V5WP4zdZVFt6NgNJktTtpK-q_WwcsDS9hF&c=5R09gYjFkp7KVUncb20-yw_2-dFASMvyebS5qEnCpenq_uzpYnfP8Q==&ch=lo_Qizgc-2DTwg34Q00YB81V66yMzg_zbWgQFJwRdcPw4cRbcV3LgA==)

name: Andie Levien (via Democrats.org)
subject: A legal update from the DNC:
body:
---
Derek, It’s Andie Levien, General Counsel for the DNC. It’s my job to oversee all legal issues related to the Party’s work, **which includes taking Trump to court.** Here’s what’s happening right now: Republicans are so worried that they can’t compete with our grassroots donor base that they’re asking the Supreme Court to change campaign finance rules to give their billionaire donors even more influence. JD Vance himself is leading the charge. So Democrats stepped in –– fighting with everything we’ve got to keep those limits in place. Last month, we argued against Trump and Vance before the Supreme Court to protect our democracy from billionaire interference. But here’s the thing: These aren’t easy cases to win. That’s why the DNC needs a surge of grassroots support to help us hand the billionaire-backed president a crucial defeat. [**So, Derek, if you’re ready to join us in our fight to beat Donald Trump and Republicans in court to ensure that the integrity of our democracy and our elections remain intact, and to elect Democrats up and down the ballot in 2026, we’re asking you to chip in $7 or whatever makes sense for you today.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-january-2026?amount=7&refcode=em_20260106_5693_fr&refcode2=5693_22930333_5YECy-&akid=5693%2E22930333%2E5YECy-) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-january-2026?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20260106_5693_fr&refcode2=5693_22930333_5YECy-&akid=5693%2E22930333%2E5YECy-)**

That’s why the DNC needs a surge of grassroots support to help us hand the billionaire-backed president a crucial defeat. - If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly. | | | - ** | - | | - | - | | - | - ** Thank you, and I’ll have more updates to share soon. Andie Andrea Levien General Counsel Democratic National Committee If you gave recently via check or mail, our system may not have yet processed your gift. We are appreciative and thank you greatly for your support.

GOP Rep. "Biden let a Chinese spy balloon fly across the country,... you think President Trump would allow that?!" Jordan exploded. "Trump demonstrates strength!"
**[Watch the live CNN clash here:](https://substack.com/redirect/fb546734-72e7-4e8d-ad7f-7d4b89607062?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**

**[Together with Health Sciences Institute](https://substack.com/redirect/8344e8df-8565-4d72-a194-7d8f0c6ba4ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# [Big Pharma Exec SPILLS truth about "Plandemic"](https://substack.com/redirect/8344e8df-8565-4d72-a194-7d8f0c6ba4ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8344e8df-8565-4d72-a194-7d8f0c6ba4ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Dear truth-seeking American,
This MAHA doctor's **[viral video](https://substack.com/redirect/8344e8df-8565-4d72-a194-7d8f0c6ba4ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** not only confirms the COVID pandemic was built on lies...
But he's holding undeniable proof the next "PLANDEMIC" is starting now.
Before it comes for your family **[SEE HIS MESSAGE HERE.](https://substack.com/redirect/8344e8df-8565-4d72-a194-7d8f0c6ba4ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[ Learn More](https://substack.com/redirect/8344e8df-8565-4d72-a194-7d8f0c6ba4ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
P.S. The new "Plandemic" will kill 600,000 mostly older Americans in 2026. **[View the 2 signs of disease - and how to protect yourself here.](https://substack.com/redirect/8344e8df-8565-4d72-a194-7d8f0c6ba4ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
* * *
# **Trump launches 'fraud' investigation of California, calls state worse than Minnesota**
President Donald Trump announced Tuesday morning a **["fraud investigation of California](https://substack.com/redirect/94b9ad61-a6ca-4b61-b698-abb636f24aba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** has begun," comparing the state unfavorably to Minnesota amid its own multibillion-dollar fraud scandal.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/94b9ad61-a6ca-4b61-b698-abb636f24aba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Broad accusation:** Trump declared on Truth Social that "California, under Governor Gavin Newscum', is more corrupt than Minnesota, if that's possible," using his nickname for Democratic Governor Gavin Newsom, though the president provided no specifics about who is conducting the investigation or what alleged fraud is being examined.
* **Minnesota comparison:** The claim contrasts California's situation with Minnesota's ongoing scandal involving billions in fraud, positioning the Golden State as potentially worse despite that state already facing federal scrutiny and large-scale DHS operations targeting fraudulent activities

Trump's signed post concluded "Thank you for your attention to this matter" without elaborating on the scope, timeline, or focus of any California investigation.
* * *
# **Trump to rally House Republicans as Venezuela operation dominates expectations**
President Trump will speak to congressional Republicans Tuesday morning at the recently renamed Trump and Kennedy Center, with lawmakers predicting the recent Venezuela military action and Nicolas Maduro's capture will **[take center stage](https://substack.com/redirect/f58f3972-d709-4f35-a5e0-4de0a1719a57?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[.](https://substack.com/redirect/f58f3972-d709-4f35-a5e0-4de0a1719a57?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/2f362037-5f1c-4df7-9566-b58735d86c77?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Event timing and scope:** Trump's scheduled 10 a.m. livestreamed address comes as House members reconvene from holiday break for an all-day strategy session on 2026 priorities, though sources told Fox News Digital they anticipate Venezuela consuming most of the discussion, with one lawmaker estimating "30 minutes on Venezuela and five on policy."
* **Expected messaging:** Florida Representative Mike Haridopolos believes Trump will walk through both the legal rationale for the operation and emphasize Maduro's international isolation, pointing out U.S., Canadian, and European Union non-recognition means "The only people who recognize him are our enemies."
* **Rally format predicted:** Texas Representative Wesley Hunt anticipated Trump covering administration achievements, the Caracas operation, codifying his policy agenda, and November midterm strategy in what one source described as a likely "rally-type speech," while another GOP member complained about delaying strategy discussions "until we get back to work."

The gathering at the renamed performing arts center follows Maduro's New York arraignment, with Democrats already characterizing the Venezuelan intervention as grounds for impeachment.
* * *
# **Corporation for Public Broadcasting officially shuts down after GOP funding cuts**
The **[Corporation for Public Broadcasting](https://substack.com/redirect/c92f368e-6a97-43d9-aeb2-4edd9a8ec408?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** officially ceased operations Monday following congressional Republican votes to eliminate taxpayer funding for the nearly 60-year-old organization that distributed federal money to PBS, NPR, and their affiliates nationwide.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c92f368e-6a97-43d9-aeb2-4edd9a8ec408?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Complete dissolution:** CPB's board of directors decided to completely dissolve the agency rather than maintain it as an inactive entity that a future Democratic administration could revive, with President and CEO Patricia Harrison stating "CPB's final act would be to protect the integrity of the public media system and the democratic values by dissolving, rather than allowing the organization to remain defunded and vulnerable to additional attacks."
* **Budget battle details:** Congress voted last July to slash approximately $9.4 billion in public media and foreign aid spending--eliminating $8.3 billion from USAID and $1.1 billion from CPB--after White House requests citing the organization's "lengthy history of anti-conservative bias," pointing to NPR CEO Katherine Maher's past description of Trump as a "fascist" and "deranged racist" plus recent PBS transgender character programming.
* **Surviving entities:** NPR and PBS continue operating independently through diverse funding including foundation grants, advertising revenue, and voluntary donations from viewers and listeners, maintaining operations despite losing federal support channeled through the now-defunct CPB.

Maher told Congress in March she regretted her earlier Trump comments as the White House pushed for the less funding that ultimately ended the Corporation for Public Broadcasting.
* * *
# **Congress unveils $174 billion spending package to avoid January shutdown**
Lawmakers released a new spending bill Monday totaling at least $174 billion that could receive a House vote this week, representing significant progress toward preventing another **[government shutdown](https://substack.com/redirect/2f79859c-c13f-4c9f-b5f8-43b8f80773ed?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** ahead of the January 30 deadline.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2f79859c-c13f-4c9f-b5f8-43b8f80773ed?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Three-bill package structure:** The bipartisan legislation bundles appropriations for commerce-justice-science ($78 billion for NASA, FBI, U.S. Marshals, and Bureau of Prisons), energy-water development ($58 billion with $25 billion for National Nuclear Security Administration including weapons modernization), and interior-environment ($38 billion for EPA, Forest Service, and Interior Department), plus over $3 billion in community project earmarks.
* **Bipartisan backing secured:** House Appropriations Chairman Tom Cole praised investments in community safety and energy infrastructure, while top Democrat Rosa DeLauro called it "a forceful rejection of draconian cuts" proposed by Trump and Republicans, with Speaker Mike Johnson framing it as avoiding a "bloated omnibus bill" to satisfy conservative fiscal hawks like Freedom Caucus Chairman Andy Harris who sees it keeping discretionary spending below last year's levels.
* **Path forward mapped:** The House Rules Committee meets Tuesday evening to advance the legislation toward Thursday's likely floor vote, with Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer signaling Democratic cooperation, though Senator Patty Murray emphasized the package ensures "Congress, not President Trump and Russ Vought, decides how taxpayer dollars are spent."

Passing this package would complete six of twelve required spending bills, with more difficult measures like defense funding still pending following the 43-day government shutdown that ended in November.

clean_body: "Americans wants a Commander-in-Chief who shows strength and takes decisive action!" | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? "Americans wants a Commander-in-Chief who shows strength and takes decisive action!" | | | Jan 6 | | | | | | | | | | | | GOP Rep. Jim Jordan for attacking President Donald Trump's actions in Venezuela and the capture of former socialist dictator Nicolas Maduro, setting the record straight on his America First agenda. "Biden let a Chinese spy balloon fly across the country,... you think President Trump would allow that?!" Jordan exploded. "Trump demonstrates strength!" | | ---|---|--- Dear truth-seeking American, This MAHA doctor's not only confirms the COVID pandemic was built on lies... But he's holding undeniable proof the next "PLANDEMIC" is starting now. Before it comes for your family P.S. The new "Plandemic" will kill 600,000 mostly older Americans in 2026. Trump launches 'fraud' investigation of California, calls state worse than Minnesota President Donald Trump announced Tuesday morning a has begun," comparing the state unfavorably to Minnesota amid its own multibillion-dollar fraud scandal. | | ---|---|--- Broad accusation: Trump declared on Truth Social that "California, under Governor Gavin Newscum', is more corrupt than Minnesota, if that's possible," using his nickname for Democratic Governor Gavin Newsom, though the president provided no specifics about who is conducting the investigation or what alleged fraud is being examined. Minnesota comparison: The claim contrasts California's situation with Minnesota's ongoing scandal involving billions in fraud, positioning the Golden State as potentially worse despite that state already facing federal scrutiny and large-scale DHS operations targeting fraudulent activities Trump's signed post concluded "Thank you for your attention to this matter" without elaborating on the scope, timeline, or focus of any California investigation. Trump to rally House Republicans as Venezuela operation dominates expectations President Trump will speak to congressional Republicans Tuesday morning at the recently renamed Trump and Kennedy Center, with lawmakers predicting the recent Venezuela military action and Nicolas Maduro's capture will Event timing and scope: Trump's scheduled 10 a.m. livestreamed address comes as House members reconvene from holiday break for an all-day strategy session on 2026 priorities, though sources told Fox News Digital they anticipate Venezuela consuming most of the discussion, with one lawmaker estimating "30 minutes on Venezuela and five on policy." Expected messaging: Florida Representative Mike Haridopolos believes Trump will walk through both the legal rationale for the operation and emphasize Maduro's international isolation, pointing out U.S., Canadian, and European Union non-recognition means "The only people who recognize him are our enemies." Rally format predicted: Texas Representative Wesley Hunt anticipated Trump covering administration achievements, the Caracas operation, codifying his policy agenda, and November midterm strategy in what one source described as a likely "rally-type speech," while another GOP member complained about delaying strategy discussions "until we get back to work." The gathering at the renamed performing arts center follows Maduro's New York arraignment, with Democrats already characterizing the Venezuelan intervention as grounds for impeachment. Corporation for Public Broadcasting officially shuts down after GOP funding cuts The officially ceased operations Monday following congressional Republican votes to eliminate taxpayer funding for the nearly 60-year-old organization that distributed federal money to PBS, NPR, and their affiliates nationwide. | | ---|---|--- Complete dissolution: CPB's board of directors decided to completely dissolve the agency rather than maintain it as an inactive entity that a future Democratic administration could revive, with President and CEO Patricia Harrison stating "CPB's final act would be to protect the integrity of the public media system and the democratic values by dissolving, rather than allowing the organization to remain defunded and vulnerable to additional attacks." Budget battle details: Congress voted last July to slash approximately $9.4 billion in public media and foreign aid spending--eliminating $8.3 billion from USAID and $1.1 billion from CPB--after White House requests citing the organization's "lengthy history of anti-conservative bias," pointing to NPR CEO Katherine Maher's past description of Trump as a "fascist" and "deranged racist" plus recent PBS transgender character programming. Surviving entities: NPR and PBS continue operating independently through diverse funding including foundation grants, advertising revenue, and voluntary donations from viewers and listeners, maintaining operations despite losing federal support channeled through the now-defunct CPB. Maher told Congress in March she regretted her earlier Trump comments as the White House pushed for the less funding that ultimately ended the Corporation for Public Broadcasting. Congress unveils $174 billion spending package to avoid January shutdown Lawmakers released a new spending bill Monday totaling at least $174 billion that could receive a House vote this week, representing significant progress toward preventing another ahead of the January 30 deadline. | | ---|---|--- Three-bill package structure: The bipartisan legislation bundles appropriations for commerce-justice-science ($78 billion for NASA, FBI, U.S. Marshals, and Bureau of Prisons), energy-water development ($58 billion with $25 billion for National Nuclear Security Administration including weapons modernization), and interior-environment ($38 billion for EPA, Forest Service, and Interior Department), plus over $3 billion in community project earmarks. Bipartisan backing secured: House Appropriations Chairman Tom Cole praised investments in community safety and energy infrastructure, while top Democrat Rosa DeLauro called it "a forceful rejection of draconian cuts" proposed by Trump and Republicans, with Speaker Mike Johnson framing it as avoiding a "bloated omnibus bill" to satisfy conservative fiscal hawks like Freedom Caucus Chairman Andy Harris who sees it keeping discretionary spending below last year's levels. Path forward mapped: The House Rules Committee meets Tuesday evening to advance the legislation toward Thursday's likely floor vote, with Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer signaling Democratic cooperation, though Senator Patty Murray emphasized the package ensures "Congress, not President Trump and Russ Vought, decides how taxpayer dollars are spent." Passing this package would complete six of twelve required spending bills, with more difficult measures like defense funding still pending following the 43-day government shutdown that ended in November. * You're currently a free subscriber to . name: Britney VanCitters
subject: Hold big polluters accountable and learn to share your story 📣
body:
https://secure.ngpvan.com/2gxiMZv_HUCVO3r0_Sx_1Q2?contactdata=LCLSvhY3NA51wz9Xe4i2maWevprkWO3szRG4DtlIyA4H8gIjjmiyJ6DMjrPwu2EshziVaGveouqU8h5IjmPFCMD5LV0FIuPeUHVWrNkumBD+0J5dNTSVhGPL5GMfxu3b [https://secure.ngpvan.com/2gxiMZv_HUCVO3r0_Sx_1Q2?contactdata=LCLSvhY3NA51wz9Xe4i2maWevprkWO3szRG4DtlIyA4H8gIjjmiyJ6DMjrPwu2EshziVaGveouqU8h5IjmPFCMD5LV0FIuPeUHVWrNkumBD+0J5dNTSVhGPL5GMfxu3b]
Hi Peter,
The science is clear: Climate change is human-caused, and it’s been impacting our communities for decades. Our neighbors are losing their homes, and sometimes even their lives, to fires, floods, and extreme heat. Cities and states are spending billions to respond to nonstop climate disasters and extreme weather events. But instead of paying their fair share for the damage they’ve caused, fossil fuel companies continue to rake in massive profits at our expense.
It’s time they’re held accountable. Join OLCV and the Make Polluters Pay coalition for a night of community building and storytelling, so you can help pass the Climate Resilience Superfund! Everyone has been impacted by climate change, so everyone has a story to tell. Can we count on you to be part of this movement?
Make Polluters Pay Storytelling Workshop
WHEN: Tuesday, January 27 from 6 to 7:30 p.m.
WHERE: on Zoom
RSVP here! [https://secure.ngpvan.com/2gxiMZv_HUCVO3r0_Sx_1Q2?contactdata=LCLSvhY3NA51wz9Xe4i2maWevprkWO3szRG4DtlIyA4H8gIjjmiyJ6DMjrPwu2EshziVaGveouqU8h5IjmPFCMD5LV0FIuPeUHVWrNkumBD+0J5dNTSVhGPL5GMfxu3b]
At this workshop, you’ll build confidence in sharing your story by learning:
* The basics of storytelling including time to practice
* Storytelling best practices and a helpful story framework
* How to use your story to advocate for change
Crafting and sharing your story to help make progress on the things you care about most is so powerful. I hope you will join us for this storytelling workshop and join our movement to hold big polluters accountable.
I hope to see you there!
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AVLCV/1/70877/images/Britney_signature-removebg-preview.png]
Britney VanCitters (she/her)
OLCV Political & Organizing Director

name: Geoff Duncan, Democrat for Georgia
subject: On this January 6th anniversary, will you share your opinion with me?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Duncan for Governor
body:
|
---

| | Hello,

I’ll be honest: I believe Donald Trump is the worst thing that ever happened to politics in our country. Since today is the 5th anniversary of the insurrection on January 6, 2021, the future of our democracy is on my mind.

I have always stood up to Trump -- including when he tried to steal our election in 2020. Now, I’m running for Georgia governor as a proud Democrat to defend our state from Trump’s extremism.

**I’m reaching out personally because I want to hear about your priorities in the upcoming election.**_[Will you answer a few quick questions about what issues matter most to you?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122782293/593436625/376261982?sc=EM_RTI_GD_20260106_FR_1A_T1&source=EM_RTI_GD_20260106_FR_1A_T1&noskip=true&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amount=10&refcode=EM_RTI_GD_20260106_FR_1A_T2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMjA3MDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzdhZTQzNjAtMThlYi1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=i_E64vlKnhQdjoeYBOhUXh7dC15EK8TxvK02Z34h77M=&emci=4112fc26-7eea-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=c7ae4360-18eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=18272505)_**** ** ** | ****Geoff Duncan, Democrat for Georgia Governor**
**Issues Survey Prepared For:** dpwillis67@gmail.com
**Questions:** 5 **| Est. Time to Complete:** 3 mins
**Question One:**
In your opinion, what is the most important reason to elect a Democratic governor in Georgia?**
---
****[BEGIN SURVEY](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122782294/593436626/376261982?sc=EM_RTI_GD_20260106_FR_1A_T1&source=EM_RTI_GD_20260106_FR_1A_T1&noskip=true&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amount=10&refcode=EM_RTI_GD_20260106_FR_1A_T2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMjA3MDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzdhZTQzNjAtMThlYi1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=i_E64vlKnhQdjoeYBOhUXh7dC15EK8TxvK02Z34h77M=&emci=4112fc26-7eea-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=c7ae4360-18eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=18272505) ****
****
Donald Trump has made it clear what MAGA priorities look like. Instead of taking on rising costs, President Trump has spent his time granting preemptive pardons to J6 conspirators.

On today’s date in 2021, Donald Trump’s lies fueled a violent mob that stormed the Capitol and attacked police officers. The last thing these fake electors deserve is a pardon.

**Trump is even trying to pardon Burt Jones, who tried to help him overturn Georgia’s election. I personally kicked Jones out of his leadership position in the legislature for it. Now I’m running to beat both of them once and for all.**

**_[I can’t win without your support, so I want to hear from you: What issues do you think our campaign should prioritize?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122782295/593436627/376261982?sc=EM_RTI_GD_20260106_FR_1A_T1&source=EM_RTI_GD_20260106_FR_1A_T1&noskip=true&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=&employer=&occupation=&amount=10&refcode=EM_RTI_GD_20260106_FR_1A_T2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMjA3MDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzdhZTQzNjAtMThlYi1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=i_E64vlKnhQdjoeYBOhUXh7dC15EK8TxvK02Z34h77M=&emci=4112fc26-7eea-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=c7ae4360-18eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=18272505)_**

Thank you so much.

\-- Geoff

name: Jena Griswold
subject: 5 years ago today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jena for Colorado
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2601-q1-jg?refcode=260106-email [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2601-q1-jg?refcode=260106-email]
Friend,
Five years ago today, our nation witnessed one of the darkest moments in modern American history.
On January 6, 2021, violent extremists, encouraged by Donald Trump, stormed the U.S. Capitol in an attempt to overturn a free and fair election. They attacked police officers, shattered windows, and tried to stop the peaceful transfer of power. They tried to silence the American people.
We must never forget what happened that day, and we must never allow it to happen again.
As Colorado’s Secretary of State, I saw firsthand how Trump’s lies and conspiracy theories threaten election workers, fuel harassment, and put our democracy at risk. I refused to back down then, and I won’t back down now.
But the truth is, the same forces that led to January 6 are still here. Extremists continue to spread disinformation, undermine confidence in elections, and push policies that strip away rights and weaken the rule of law. That’s why this campaign matters.
If we want leaders who will defend the Constitution, protect our elections, and uphold the rule of law, we have to build that future together. And we have to do it now.
If you’re able, will you chip in today to help me continue the work of safeguarding democracy as Colorado’s next Attorney General? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2601-q1-jg?refcode=260106-email]
January 6 was a wake-up call for the nation. What we continue to do will shape our future.
Thank you for standing with me, today and every day, in defense of our democracy and nation.
With gratitude,
Jena Griswold
Follow Jena Online:
https://www.facebook.com/Jenaforcolorado/ [https://www.facebook.com/Jenaforcolorado/] https://twitter.com/JenaGriswold [https://twitter.com/JenaGriswold] https://www.instagram.com/jenagriswold/ [https://www.instagram.com/jenagriswold/] https://www.threads.net/@jenagriswold [https://www.threads.net/@jenagriswold] https://bsky.app/profile/jenagriswold.bsky.social [https://bsky.app/profile/jenagriswold.bsky.social]

Jena for Colorado

Louisville, CO 80027
United States

Standing for Truth has never been more important. Our guest Jennifer Sey knows this firsthand. She’s a seven-time member of the
U.S. women’s national gymnastics team. In 1986 she was awarded the title of women’s all-around national champion. She had a long,
successful career as a business executive with Levi Strauss as their Chief Marketing Officer and Brand President. She says she
blew up her own career there for speaking out against the COVID school lockdowns. Now she is the founder of XX-XY Athletics – the
only brand standing up for female athletes and the protection of women’s sports. Hear Jennifer's incredible story here. Like,
subscribe and share.

5 Patriotic Resolutions for America's 250th Anniversary

The best resolutions aren’t the ones that require a total personality overhaul and color-coded binders. The best ones are simple,
doable, and meaningful – especially when you’re raising kids and trying to build a strong home in a world that feels like it
changes by the hour. We can choose what we celebrate. We can choose what we teach. We can choose what kind of citizens we’re
raising around our kitchen table.

So as we step into 2026, let’s lean in. Not with guilt, not with perfectionism, and definitely not with another resolution that
makes you feel like you’re already behind by the middle of January. Let’s go with “less guilt, more grit” – five patriotic New
Year’s resolutions that add a little stars-and-stripes to your everyday life, rally the family around what matters, and help your
kids fall in love with the country they call home.

clean_body: How will you stand for Truth this year? We're talking with a courageous athlete and business woman about what she has done and sharing patriotic New Year's resolutions to consider. If you are having trouble viewing this email click here to view in your browser. [] [] [] January 6, 2026 • Newsletter Standing for Truth: XX-XY Athletics Moms for America Weekly Podcast [] [] Moms for America Weekly Podcast [] Standing for Truth has never been more important. Our guest Jennifer Sey knows this firsthand. She’s a seven-time member of the U.S. women’s national gymnastics team. In 1986 she was awarded the title of women’s all-around national champion. She had a long, successful career as a business executive with Levi Strauss as their Chief Marketing Officer and Brand President. She says she blew up her own career there for speaking out against the COVID school lockdowns. Now she is the founder of XX-XY Athletics – the only brand standing up for female athletes and the protection of women’s sports. Hear Jennifer's incredible story here. Like, subscribe and share. View the Podcast [] Listen to the Podcast [] Listen to the Podcast on Spotify [] Moms for America Weekly Blog [] [] 5 Patriotic Resolutions for America's 250th Anniversary The best resolutions aren’t the ones that require a total personality overhaul and color-coded binders. The best ones are simple, doable, and meaningful – especially when you’re raising kids and trying to build a strong home in a world that feels like it changes by the hour. We can choose what we celebrate. We can choose what we teach. We can choose what kind of citizens we’re raising around our kitchen table. So as we step into 2026, let’s lean in. Not with guilt, not with perfectionism, and definitely not with another resolution that makes you feel like you’re already behind by the middle of January. Let’s go with “less guilt, more grit” – five patriotic New Year’s resolutions that add a little stars-and-stripes to your everyday life, rally the family around what matters, and help your kids fall in love with the country they call home. Read the Blog [] [] In Truth & Liberty, Kimberly Fletcher Founder and President Moms for America® [] [] [] [] [] [] [] [] [] [] Copyright © 2026 Moms for America, All rights reserved. body:
While public service is David’s passion, his life is his family. Anyone who knows David knows that his proudest role is being a husband and a father. | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvmfuXmYYW_FOYjb_3v61emWuNXO-wS3-aHqPhXdS_0AOJubYzSqDcubjA-pAHVBC87_XW0v_Yj-7XBxFQdfvbTote4rd4-pSpW07ywkKltv5Jlyw6Mt11o7Pqa63jROGG0WPT9ay6JOjgymT9fNzZDvX4h0v8yZUSCfBGKfhio6DEV2jTqTsxBZmieVc3h6qyCbFVDAg8eYbZ_-4QcA70aZNb4l6PZOkSK6TRHv6qn-WqVz2w-M700jT6XhYapShpMCcefRyXWKMLnFtI7DIoPwOiWSF6qpCqslKrg7SFSXSFf2awXSUe_fjq-LWfsFvi1LU3_6hGNAuu8PLumPQDw22o_GCPbNKww4tlF22prkBHHsfn253Rf6wBe99nE7F3ksyuWhJldGa8qr1rZRvaXe1Z9YoqUF681zHgs1-UGRuXa7Up_4Vk28lXS0SjoYsw/4n2/h5es1eiBSv-4SFccy4pKSQ/h0/h001.qmJ5i969iNsvDYlgiVpmBNX0uJnE4dBEEpk038Jre0o)

Hi Peter –

This is Ericka, David’s wife.

In just a moment, I’m going to ask you to **_[make a contribution to David’s campaign for governor](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvmfuXmYYW_FOYjb_3v61emrCPVlLR4wHrkJuqVQvzQJSxocscaoZLLVohVBZBsI5du6nyGt5HBD6DAzGztFTl0m_JX-ZFITF0bSfnbigCXHDzisiBytsBlXo3jevpdmGp3yetOzlEDc-D5v68wXf6qx98jUAJMFuNHXqUCWeIj7yTA_7EyckXb8x2ELQ0GcmOye3UeKJsC0t9ykdIBIFyinMD2PKy1wwPpRz_FKI1RPRejIxKEXRybrSupgnFThlGFQ3TUq4WEFMxvLarLF3nBn4H4dwbRHBs-yiWumRO6auQHZveWUbqcGA791XlCGyHwLz-UdE6jGdmXL7y10yXSvcgh4_nmNtOYKekHTNNsNl7jhYhFdhUoiZS954NoM0iJIkN2QP9iFyL7CtGt-2LFY7sXFZGqBiNlXkhyWTZ3gmw401e_2MVD_-Zzvu70afA/4n2/h5es1eiBSv-4SFccy4pKSQ/h1/h001.53C8eEhPgneGqNu98oK2u8tGrBXqMah3EjNtDsP_Bds)_** – but before I do, let me introduce you to the David I know.

* * *
Choosing to marry David was the easiest decision I ever made. In so many ways, David’s work as a leader is rooted in the same love, integrity, and responsibility that guide him as a husband and father.

In 2020, amid a global pandemic, David made history — becoming the youngest and first Black County Executive ever elected in Milwaukee County history and he got sworn in right in front of our home.

Amidst all the uncertainty of the pandemic, I saw David get up **every day** with a determination to make good on the promise he made to the people of Milwaukee County: to build a community where people could feel safe, healthy, afford a home, and find a good-paying job.

And every day, despite a pandemic, catastrophic floods, or budget challenges, he has shown up and taken care of business.

**Because that’s the kind of person David is.**

[](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvmfuXmYYW_FOYjb_3v61emrCPVlLR4wHrkJuqVQvzQJSxocscaoZLLVohVBZBsI5c9y08VFCjJqtwo5zT2fH70OgW5ioQSXflRza2rDn7tEclAwq8W9sdsbJgGBCm8mgMPcMeDDr-h299xKeu8IMhmgbifbQCG8xgcPNDhw8ESl1rTQXthcbmueNw3D-p59WaYC-WO7fAFLVoZv8r61zApZd2wQVuaeB0PfoygbaTBQVmhUDDIm9DWhE0w1hL8Be2q4QSeolyv56zuTmr3Z1uMmDevu7n7b0eu3MD1GMsct4P-6RFvIDGd9FE_hV7_u_GU6yuebs239Xl-TrwUHbjJE0NNjySek_Q6M4IZbK-khpmtpMzMlkfV4IlqyRDnOnu1zM_OyRbCkvdsjp_vLdVrx0AJR7Rcck4Ilfgz9CLEhDcNPFeBrPuPjfiO3QhPRXg/4n2/h5es1eiBSv-4SFccy4pKSQ/h2/h001.dazxwhsRKtktMzcTTMVZSjGS872HX1drwUJAacdC6pE)
**Behind the scenes, he’s no different**. He’s an outstanding father to our 3 daughters, and no matter how packed his schedule as County Executive is, he always finds time in his day to be “dad.”

While public service is David’s passion, his life is his family. Anyone who knows David knows that his proudest role is being a husband and a father. Our family is what grounds him in the everyday hopes, challenges, and decisions facing working families across the state. His lived experience isn’t just a story — it’s the lens he brings to every policy, every budget, every fight for progress.

David’s dedication to Wisconsin’s working families is personal.

Choosing to marry David was the easiest decision I ever made. I hope that every Wisconsinite feels that same clarity when electing our next governor. Until then, please consider donating to David’s campaign for Governor.

**_[If you’re ready to help elect David as our next Governor, chip in $10 – or whatever you can – to power this campaign.](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvmfuXmYYW_FOYjb_3v61emrCPVlLR4wHrkJuqVQvzQJSxocscaoZLLVohVBZBsI5c9y08VFCjJqtwo5zT2fH70EdQLQnCxbBH3U1NjCSt-jZjAmGXef-V67twIFuxuFyCHm0qonV4bJWc8l2tRE_UUC523I2utVCV3PM_SOT3duQ4tB1gTbduPCPq5UnXzED12OqWD6OUJ2cQ2rhvi1b4QTH6dW2yhjL0tlTFQV_7VCSsVZHbj3UNZl8PGUyD8gx3YnB37osnmYKg1_xBnA7ZxkQ1ZRvKVAfgXg4tJG-9TBbUPxBogPN9reqgtIlcBWkKcm-2dxq_kmt89fAuac-YQaQmV6kEGynmQw0MaY3UKZ1XHjd52c9e3CKSyB32RvBFA3cvjDmSvdNgQAmxt-GbruzRJW9bT1cmNNSGcKxQLReM0fZmKnoRiVvCdgXFosyU/4n2/h5es1eiBSv-4SFccy4pKSQ/h3/h001.lGzbFDE3tnJagJyLjtO-3OQm_eUyhT8k3_SViYV5w5U)_**

name: Justin Streiff, Turning Point USA
subject: Charlie’s final book wasn’t about politics...
body:

| | Fellow Patriot,
---
| | Charlie Kirk built a movement for truth -- but his final message pointed to something eternal.

It wasn't about politics... or elections... or even about the culture war.

His final book was deeply personal, and unlike anything Charlie had written before...

**It was about the Sabbath. **

About sacred rest. Reconnecting with God.

Charlie wrote this book because he knew what America was missing... and what he nearly lost himself.

He found that in a world addicted to busyness, noise, and scrolling...

_**The most radical act of resistance is to STOP. **_

To rest. To reflect. To return to God.

This book is his blueprint for how we do that -- as individuals, as families, and as a nation.

🔥 You'll discover how Charlie learned to honor the Sabbath in a culture that never stops
🔥 How setting aside just one day a week gave him clarity, peace, and strength
🔥 Why he believed this message could spark a spiritual revival across America

I know he would've wanted you to have this book.

**When you give today to Turning Point USA, you 're not just supporting the movement Charlie built -- you're helping pass on the message he felt most urgently in his final months. **

**[And we’ll ship you a copy of Charlie’s final book straight to your door.](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=13f40409f2704a0e9bca41727aceac48&_e=x7ss4dsxl1b2bZYQ9OMrRJCXnnJNMrkO-nHPQSVIMAgNJdAVriiyl-sKMrtSlSde356pZFBccP2TEYSTLWV4baryF9W4XB3KYd5mrtQ6-XPmVtNmfQhUXsbwovk9rEMqZbGyj0fl15YxVUsAtKi8PlWrbo4GrSmvJptcxI4tniFvn42CfiAgQEg9WnHTJ2zRtVQMIDYBjaGgw3feAGLS6xtBDePA1fVeLBES1dSQVx_qnZ5wHK01Ff7U5anT1hc3Q_4gT2PwG4nrOrqQhUVXknDCzAUAlkSBlTi2RLzhzejfDmxXqjsD8HyYNZ5SmtY7kZBYTkgmOft-s-NnKvyiFAi3eC4syU-dDEkMJjjvSPMLJ2VolPOvF0rBQw5uPNxjDv-bvog3mmF_iU3doYN2fgMWzxehkLwGrGZH8jLLE5DwqfyW53K1OdYNEFwzmXdTiIPGsyCukwgLk_l9R1JnkiAo2axq7va4MVvDpDwtmXWZGBOzEELMsv89jgXeTvmVP1d0zB_omus-AYSkYSJegrp_t1CVCUnrdxXMp30IBT0Vh0ye_zsq3gb8W1iza5n5idSchmrT_ZZtP5M-vWy3_Pf4jzfYxc6rwe7jv5h57G096va4Cb2MXTyadonKyjpyl2K8CutZXsEd-1y3VO7wHINRVABvXCvflqpormjDYW3WsrR10S_2lRl0KCkg_xPJ)**

name: Sen. Jeff Bridges
subject: $100,000 in Q4. $250K+ cash on hand. Let’s go
body:
Because of your support, we didn’t just finish the year strong, we crushed it. We closed out 2025 with a huge milestone—over $100,000 raised

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/a12e65f6af4e/get-more-smarter-on-colorados-budget-10372392?e=b48bb2b4f4)
---
[|
---](https://bridgesforcolorado.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ba078768558df9fe7d8559d49&id=64676e06bc&e=b48bb2b4f4)
| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Hi there,
Happy New Year! And thank you.
Because of your support, we didn’t just finish the year strong, we crushed it. **We closed out 2025 with a huge milestone—over $100,000 raised in the final quarter.** That came from more than 250 individual supporters, with most giving $250 or less.
This incredible people-powered finish brings our total to just under $350,000 raised overall, with well over a quarter million dollars cash-on-hand as we head into the new year.
That matters. It means we’re entering 2026 with real momentum, the resources to communicate statewide, and the credibility to show this campaign is serious, disciplined, and built to win.
---
[|
---](https://bridgesforcolorado.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ba078768558df9fe7d8559d49&id=df172a355e&e=b48bb2b4f4)
| |
More importantly, it means we can keep making the case for a Treasurer who invests Colorado dollars in Colorado—in housing that’s actually affordable, infrastructure that creates good-paying jobs, and renewable energy that lowers utility bills while fighting global warming.
I’m deeply grateful for your trust, your investment, and the many conversations along the way. There’s a lot more work ahead, but we’re right where we need to be.
In the meantime, please enjoy the pic of Kit’s first time dancing to Sweet Caroline. [Video on Instagram](https://bridgesforcolorado.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ba078768558df9fe7d8559d49&id=c7474c8529&e=b48bb2b4f4). 😂
---
| | Talk soon,
P.S. Stay tuned—we’ve got a big endorsement coming next week!
---
|

clean_body: Because of your support, we didn’t just finish the year strong, we crushed it. We closed out 2025 with a huge milestone—over $100,000 raised | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Hi there, Happy New Year! And thank you. Because of your support, we didn’t just finish the year strong, we crushed it. We closed out 2025 with a huge milestone—over $100,000 raised in the final quarter. That came from more than 250 individual supporters, with most giving $250 or less. This incredible people-powered finish brings our total to just under $350,000 raised overall, with well over a quarter million dollars cash-on-hand as we head into the new year. That matters. It means we’re entering 2026 with real momentum, the resources to communicate statewide, and the credibility to show this campaign is serious, disciplined, and built to win. | | More importantly, it means we can keep making the case for a Treasurer who invests Colorado dollars in Colorado—in housing that’s actually affordable, infrastructure that creates good-paying jobs, and renewable energy that lowers utility bills while fighting global warming. I’m deeply grateful for your trust, your investment, and the many conversations along the way. There’s a lot more work ahead, but we’re right where we need to be. In the meantime, please enjoy the pic of Kit’s first time dancing to Sweet Caroline. . 😂 | | Talk soon, P.S. Stay tuned—we’ve got a big endorsement coming next week! | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright (C) 2026 Bridges for Colorado Treasurer. All rights reserved.

name: Letter from Team Trump
subject: Our anniversary is days away
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Never Surrender, Inc
body:
Friend, don’t forget to sign Trump’s 1-year anniversary card.
| | | |
# **ANNIVERSARY ALERT!**

---

Just days remain until Trump’s 1-year anniversary as President.
DON’T SHARE THIS WITH TRUMP BUT have you signed his card yet?

[SIGN BEFORE YOU FORGET >](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
[](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
**_President Trump received a beautiful message from you once and we’re confident that he’d love to see one from you AGAIN!_**

That’s why YOU were one of the very few supporters selected to sign Trump’s card and add a personal message of praise, encouragement, or even constructive feedback.

[SIGN THE CARD](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
Don Jr., Kristi Noem, Karoline Leavitt, Marco Rubio and many other Trump allies have already signed this card.
**So, what’s the hold up?**

[SIGN THE CARD](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
Your name will be proudly displayed on the official card delivered straight to President Trump. We’re confident that the boss will be devastated if he doesn’t see your name at the top!

[Donate & Sign the Card](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
Now that we’ve officially entered a critical midterm year, we need your help to keep fighting.
**_[Chip in any amount today to stand with President Trump as we mark this milestone and continue the fight ahead.>](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)_**
Thank you for your unwavering support.
Team Trump

\---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Team Deluzio
Subject: I want to be direct with you: this fight isn't over
To: dpwillis67@gmail.com

Derek,
Tomorrow marks five years since the January 6th insurrection—a dark day when we saw the heart of American democracy attacked.
A crowd of traitors stormed the U.S. Capitol, put lives in danger, brutalized Capitol Police officers, and tried to overthrow our government. What happened that day was unconscionable. And the threat to our democracy didn’t end when the mob was cleared from the building.
**I want to be direct with you: this fight isn’t over—not even close.**
Since Donald Trump returned to office, he and his allies have put tremendous pressure on the rule of law. He’s flouted the Constitution—abusing his pardon power, seeking retribution against his foes through law enforcement, and even threatening me and several of my colleagues with death for stating the law, for crying out loud.
No matter what Trump or his congressional allies try to do, nothing will deter me from standing up for our democracy, defending our freedoms, and doing my job for the people of Western PA. Quitting just isn’t in our vocabulary here.
**_[If you’re able, please chip in today to help me continue the fight to defend our democracy and our Constitution →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122786049/593450652/-1542703181?refcode=email_010626_fwd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZjI5NTQzMy0xZGViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=667RZJp-kFKo7IAxteIXNNvoWWZ1jVZ5CyKcMw2o1aI=)_**

Criminals who stormed our Capitol and beat the crap out of cops should be serving time, not being served pardons on Day One of Trump’s presidency.
But those are Donald Trump’s priorities. Not bringing down costs, not his oath to the Constitution—but pandering to criminals and cop beaters.
**That behavior isn’t patriotic, Derek. It’s weak, and it’s pathetic.**
I first swore an oath to defend our Constitution at 17 years old, when I entered the Naval Academy. I swore that same oath again when I was elected to represent Pennsylvania in Congress.
That oath is sacred—and it doesn’t expire. It’s why I’ve stood up to Donald Trump and anyone who continues to undermine our democracy, threaten our rights, and put their own power ahead of the rule of law.
America is worth fighting for. I will always defend our freedoms and protect our Constitution.

body:
Seth is in Congress to fight for the middle class and those working hard to join it. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://act.sethmagaziner.com/p/9_n7o1XMskam_GoKXFDAdg2?unsubscribedata=zLBVXaGMqb5FC3NJDsmR7Nz%2BdAdd%2BukX%2BOt35qI7NtYz2QFWjfZcm9axiUiM3MFqj9bVY8mYGgZDlEj2V4%2Fdh4ZvTB9M6nAqHWRmpxR4AtXOe9knuw7GepTuIBezvBaqsz73QMlonitUQBRK2UcKxSav2GfplQf%2F1jdZy6u9DSna29BW5SdtDjS%2Blqf95cspaQYb3%2Fcmxux5CIx5E%2FqTcnW3F6S20JdlyqPNYfmQpkMvCGNgRCH37cbc6zm%2F3XqFHFTPG%2FaZdj6gjXjzM0NAaK7QHZEqhGA1HdB8QiCEh0FAWtN3ZjcpXwAc2jYi5Bq7oV72Yjp5erps22IaRvkbFdY32O3qFG0QsC%2FNyQMBEC%2BzZkbZslqN0JEef5JLJ9%2FnJOV9Hqh3YvW1M%2FGUyKRV%2Bb9Xmzyn0Yj9DMPbgPmO0yUuSN8G254BXrTX3wBJEKNy)

| |
---
Peter,

Today marks five years since we witnessed a coordinated, violent assault on the United States Capitol and the law enforcement officers sworn to protect it. **It was a day defined by lawlessness, fueled by lies, and designed to tear down the very foundations of our Country.**

But we must be clear: what happened on January 6th five years ago did not end when the glass was swept up, or the insurrectionists were cleared from the halls of Congress.

The integrity of our elections is the foundation of our democracy. We cannot allow the history of January 6th to be erased or rewritten.

And now, I have an opponent who didn’t just watch the events of January 6th unfold, but participated in that attempt to undermine our democratic process. By siding with those who sought to throw out legal votes and subvert the rule of law, he showed a dangerous disregard for the democratic principles our nation was founded on.

I am fighting every day to fortify our system against those who believe they are above the law. We cannot afford to be complacent while the bedrock of our nation is under constant siege by partisan extremism.

I refuse to accept a future where political violence and subversion are the norms. We must build a government that earns the faith of its people by standing firmly for the rule of law and the sanctity of the vote.

The work of protecting our democracy is never finished. And we must never forget the courage of the officers who stood their ground that day. Thank you for standing with me in this fight.

– Seth Magaziner

body:
| | | | Trisha Calvarese is the Democrat taking on Lauren Boebert in Colorado's 4th congressional district. Thank you for your crucial support for Trisha. However [if you'd like to unsubscribe,](https://visitor.constantcontact.com/do?p=un&m=001XvBc3bsHgRWXLyJfHoB0-w%3D&ch=005337f2-e4bf-11f0-bd14-0242b661c010&ca=8505e009-63db-4666-ad88-16a7aa7e79a1) you can do so at the link at the bottom of this email.
---
---
| | [](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001H32fyGbnXIiUkPn6OeZOS86Ql48Zc0OL-cvGeO97V_yYLp81-eUEW0GjcPEg1eEYee31ckqX0LPpfykhYn9vNOS2t2AXlC2nBYExHowF1X08KM1_Znq0dEBREa293WmPZMWzZoWg6dZKkQm3hSbRy2G7xqQBNiz6UrHbU58FzKPr6E8DZLdxDrwn2Br1Wu7E0L2ShEJ0j1Mpp8NQLGg3XQ==&c=1FilrPv7BEzjIYZio0cgs0LIfh2NVGEfJv-s4gAgbBAFFvO9EMhP-Q==&ch=t4o1REbdI7ya3LyD9nY0O3RWhKLdYVHmekG5DSwopezWMlS7-gcruA==)
---
| Peter,
I'll never forget where I was on January 6th, 2021.
At that time, I was with the U.S. labor movement working remotely due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
I was deep in my work when my phone started to light up with text messages. I looked down to see a message: "They're storming the Capitol."
I looked at the news and online and was horrified by what I saw. Not far from where I was, officers were being beaten with American flags, the windows of our Capitol were bashed in, violent insurrectionists marched through the Capitol rotunda.
The news anchor flashed to the tweets of Congresswoman Lauren Boebert, who was live tweeting the location of Nancy Pelosi, as if inviting the mob inside the capitol to assassinate her:
---
| [](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001H32fyGbnXIiUkPn6OeZOS86Ql48Zc0OL-cvGeO97V_yYLp81-eUEW0GjcPEg1eEYee31ckqX0LPpfykhYn9vNOS2t2AXlC2nBYExHowF1X08KM1_Znq0dEBREa293WmPZMWzZoWg6dZKkQm3hSbRy2G7xqQBNiz6UrHbU58FzKPr6E8DZLdxDrwn2Br1Wu7EwN7h4caxzzLF1BSnE3TNuQ==&c=1FilrPv7BEzjIYZio0cgs0LIfh2NVGEfJv-s4gAgbBAFFvO9EMhP-Q==&ch=t4o1REbdI7ya3LyD9nY0O3RWhKLdYVHmekG5DSwopezWMlS7-gcruA==)
---
At that moment, it seemed like our nation was on the brink of collapse.
But as the smoke settled and Joe Biden was finally officially sworn in, I was relieved that the chaos was finally over.
The next day, Jan. 7th, Speaker Pelosi addressed the labor movement on a virtual call. I'll never forget her warning to us: Buckle up, buttercup. This is only the beginning.
It wasn't until 2 years later that I would fully understand just how right she was.
On the second anniversary of Jan. 6 in 2023, Republicans were electing the next Speaker of the House. The vote turned into a historic debacle, with the same MAGA extremists who were complicit in provoking the violent insurrection forcing 15 votes before Republicans finally bent to their will and elected Kevin McCarthy.
An image from that day is seared into my memory, a perfect encapsulation of the extremist wing of the Republican Party gaining complete control, and almost gloating about it on the anniversary of their insurrection against our government:
---
| [](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001H32fyGbnXIiUkPn6OeZOS86Ql48Zc0OL-cvGeO97V_yYLp81-eUEW0GjcPEg1eEYee31ckqX0LPpfykhYn9vNOS2t2AXlC2nBYExHowF1X08KM1_Znq0dEBREa293WmPZMWzZoWg6dZKkQm3hSbRy2G7xqQBNiz6UrHbU58FzKPr6E8DZLdxDrwn2Br1Wu7EkbcRSluFApxdVkPrOXAbOw==&c=1FilrPv7BEzjIYZio0cgs0LIfh2NVGEfJv-s4gAgbBAFFvO9EMhP-Q==&ch=t4o1REbdI7ya3LyD9nY0O3RWhKLdYVHmekG5DSwopezWMlS7-gcruA==)
---
MAGA extremists Matt Gaetz, Marjorie Taylor Greene, and Lauren Boebert being begged by their Republican colleagues to end the chaos of that day.
---
It was at this moment that I finally understood what Speaker Pelosi had warned about; the MAGA extremists were far from defeated, in fact, they were winning. And it was only going to get worse.
Fast forward to today, and we all see the violence, chaos, and lawlessness that MAGA has wrought on our country
That's why I'm running to defeat Lauren Boebert. Although she has escaped justice for her part in the insurrection, if I defeat her at the ballot box, it will be poetic justice. And there is reason to hope: Out of the MAGA extremists in that photo, Gaetz and MTG are gone, only Boebert remains in Congress.
And Boebert's more vulnerable than ever. She is only in Congress because of 546 votes, and last time I ran against her, I had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in the history of this district.
[If you're ready to finally turn the page on this dark chapter of American history and bring justice to Lauren Boebert for her part in inciting a violent insurrection against our government, will you join our campaign with a contribution of $25, $15, or whatever you can spare? ](https://eub669hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001H32fyGbnXIiUkPn6OeZOS86Ql48Zc0OL-cvGeO97V_yYLp81-eUEW0GjcPEg1eEYW9eflGKScFg23plkiyzOGAguIf9iOUIL3MZR7Zm9Me4DKccgL97nF9PgamMi__h-FVbZBURoGSofhGAH6K4iFjLn0uYfyCTTmsF1o8wZiPeplnCEzDpS6Lk80aF-44k0mVtGm83N2C_mmUCr2y6syqsIMnEBbvOZ&c=1FilrPv7BEzjIYZio0cgs0LIfh2NVGEfJv-s4gAgbBAFFvO9EMhP-Q==&ch=t4o1REbdI7ya3LyD9nY0O3RWhKLdYVHmekG5DSwopezWMlS7-gcruA==)
---

clean_body: | | | | Trisha Calvarese is the Democrat taking on Lauren Boebert in Colorado's 4th congressional district. Thank you for your crucial support for Trisha. However you can do so at the link at the bottom of this email. | | == | Peter, I'll never forget where I was on January 6th, 2021. At that time, I was with the U.S. labor movement working remotely due to the COVID-19 pandemic. I was deep in my work when my phone started to light up with text messages. I looked down to see a message: "They're storming the Capitol." I looked at the news and online and was horrified by what I saw. Not far from where I was, officers were being beaten with American flags, the windows of our Capitol were bashed in, violent insurrectionists marched through the Capitol rotunda. The news anchor flashed to the tweets of Congresswoman Lauren Boebert, who was live tweeting the location of Nancy Pelosi, as if inviting the mob inside the capitol to assassinate her: | == At that moment, it seemed like our nation was on the brink of collapse. But as the smoke settled and Joe Biden was finally officially sworn in, I was relieved that the chaos was finally over. The next day, Jan. 7th, Speaker Pelosi addressed the labor movement on a virtual call. I'll never forget her warning to us: Buckle up, buttercup. This is only the beginning. It wasn't until 2 years later that I would fully understand just how right she was. On the second anniversary of Jan. 6 in 2023, Republicans were electing the next Speaker of the House. The vote turned into a historic debacle, with the same MAGA extremists who were complicit in provoking the violent insurrection forcing 15 votes before Republicans finally bent to their will and elected Kevin McCarthy. An image from that day is seared into my memory, a perfect encapsulation of the extremist wing of the Republican Party gaining complete control, and almost gloating about it on the anniversary of their insurrection against our government: | == MAGA extremists Matt Gaetz, Marjorie Taylor Greene, and Lauren Boebert being begged by their Republican colleagues to end the chaos of that day. It was at this moment that I finally understood what Speaker Pelosi had warned about; the MAGA extremists were far from defeated, in fact, they were winning. And it was only going to get worse. Fast forward to today, and we all see the violence, chaos, and lawlessness that MAGA has wrought on our country That's why I'm running to defeat Lauren Boebert. Although she has escaped justice for her part in the insurrection, if I defeat her at the ballot box, it will be poetic justice. And there is reason to hope: Out of the MAGA extremists in that photo, Gaetz and MTG are gone, only Boebert remains in Congress. And Boebert's more vulnerable than ever. She is only in Congress because of 546 votes, and last time I ran against her, I had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in the history of this district. == If you've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: | | | == | == | == | | | == | == | == | == | Today marks five years since the Jan 6. insurrection, too long for those who incited it to escape justice. In 2026, we have a chance to finally bring justice and kick Lauren Boebert out of Congress for good. I hope you'll join me. In Solidarity, Trisha Calvarese Democrat for CO-04 | | == | | | == Mail checks to: Trisha 4 CO 9609 S University Blvd., PO Box 631322 Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado | | | | 9609 S University Blvd, | Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 US | |

name: James Talarico
subject: $5 to keep us on the air
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Talarico for Texas
body:
#
Peter —
I shared our first TV ad yesterday, and our seven-figure ad buy starts today.
We need to get this ad in front of as many Texans as possible. We are up against previous statewide and federal candidates with near-universal name ID.
All the polling suggests that once Texans know me and hear my message, they are ready to join this movement.
But in a state as big as Texas, we still have a lot of work to do to get this message in front of voters. And that requires significant resources.
**[So I need your help — not just to keep it on the air but to expand our reach even bigger. Will you please contribute $5 or anything you can to get our new ad in front of as many voters as possible?](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-2026q1.AKJ3HX&refcode=em260106-1-ak&t=1&akid=371%2E1347274%2EKW9r41)**
[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-2026q1.AKJ3HX&refcode=em260106-1-ak&t=2&akid=371%2E1347274%2EKW9r41)
We may be the underdogs, but we’ve got a message and movement that can win.
James

Paid for by Talarico for Texas
Talarico for Texas
PO Box 14508
Austin, TX 78761
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you need to update your contact information, [**you can submit this form.**](https://act.jamestalarico.com/signup/update-info/?t=3&akid=371%2E1347274%2EKW9r41) Looking for Team Talarico merch? [**Check out our online store!**](https://store.jamestalarico.com?utm_source=em_footer&utm_campaign=em_footer) This is a grassroots movement to take back Texas for working people. [**Please consider making a contribution to support our fight today.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jt-em-launch?refcode=em_footer&t=4&refcode2=371_1347274_KW9r41&akid=371%2E1347274%2EKW9r41)
James is grateful to have you in our movement. We try to send only the most important updates and opportunities to participate via email, but if you want to, you can [unsubscribe here](https://act.jamestalarico.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=5&akid=371%2E1347274%2EKW9r41).

| | [](https://substack..jaUoV9Q-bESt7Ev7TOUvhwfW4tiLleP3VyZKIloXCz4?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/patrickmurphypa/p/the-10-geopolitical-shifts-that-defined?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

If 2025 felt different, it's because it was.
For decades, globalization ran on a straightforward assumption: manufacture where it's cheapest, ship where it's fastest, and deal with politics later. In 2025, that assumption finally broke down. Governments stopped treating economics and security as separate issues.
Conflict continued across multiple regions, but its effects were broader than the battlefield. Cyber and space became openly contested domains. Industrial policy shifted from a niche concern to a core element of national security. Corporations had to navigate volatile supply chains, labor disruptions, and inflation that proved systemic rather than temporary.
And yet, the global economy proved more resilient than many expected. Capital markets adjusted. Governments and businesses invested in redundancy, continuity, and strategic capacity rather than assuming stability would return on its own.
Below are some of the most notable geopolitical and economic developments that defined 2025.
### **1\. U.S. - Venezuela**
2026 is already seeing the consequences of decisions made in 2025. Few cases show that more clearly than Venezuela.
Last year, U.S.-Venezuela policy shifted from a familiar sanctions-and-rhetoric approach to something more strategic and risky. The Trump administration leaned into what it openly framed as a corollary to the Monroe Doctrine: the Western Hemisphere as a core security zone, not just a diplomatic concern. Sanctions remained the baseline, but enforcement evolved. Financial pressure gave way to physical disruption--boat strikes, tanker seizures, and maritime interdictions aimed at choking off Venezuela's shadow oil trade.
At the same time, the U.S. kept selective channels open. Prisoner swaps happened. Chevron received a tightly constrained license. The result was a two-track policy: maximum pressure paired with narrow, transactional exceptions. Venezuela tried to extract oil revenue while giving as little ground as possible, framing U.S. actions as coercion.
What makes this shift consequential is the signal it sends. Maritime enforcement against Venezuelan oil doubled as a test case for disrupting sanction-evasion networks tied to Russia, Iran, and China. Venezuela became a proving ground.
By the end of 2025, U.S. policy was no longer just about Maduro's legitimacy. It was about energy flows, great-power competition, and control closer to home. In 2026, we're watching this all play out in real time.
If my work resonates with you, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..onxBkXwQktrZrQQGTumqTAo3Zkop0YLXoU1To6MGmck?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=183690633)
### **2\. Cyber, Space & Gray-Zone Conflict: The New Battlegrounds**
In 2025, much of global competition had moved into places that don't look like traditional battlefields. Cyber networks, satellites, financial systems, and shipping lanes became central arenas of pressure.
Russia, China, Iran, and North Korea used cyberattacks, disinformation, and gray-zone tactics to probe defenses and gain leverage below the threshold of war. Critical infrastructure was repeatedly targeted, not always to cause immediate damage, but to establish access for future crises. 85% of our critical infrastructure is in the private sector; a cyber breach costs an average of $4.8 million; cyber crime has become the 3rd largest economy in the world. Many small to medium-sized businesses cannot survive a breach.
Space also became more contested. Satellites supporting communications, navigation, and military operations were increasingly treated as strategic assets, prompting quieter investments in space defense.
Gray-zone pressure extended into the physical world as well. Maritime harassment and hybrid disruptions affected shipping from the Red Sea to the South China Sea, raising costs and reshaping trade routes, with some routes now costing 300% more (40% of the world's shipping lanes go through the Red Sea).
### **3\. China, Taiwan & the Indo-Pacific**
Competition between the U.S. and China increasingly focused on the waters around Taiwan and the sea lanes that carry much of the world's trade. Beijing applied steady military pressure aimed at changing the balance of power over time, rather than triggering open conflict. Washington responded with deterrence, working to make any use of force prohibitively costly.
What made this rivalry especially consequential was how tightly security and economics were linked. Taiwan plays an outsized role in global semiconductor production, meaning any disruption would reverberate through technology, manufacturing, and financial markets almost immediately. As a result, chips and supply chains became central national-security concerns. As a result, governments, particularly the United States, are pursuing more public-private partnerships, highlighted by tens of billions of public investment with the CHIPS ACT that has triggered a corresponding private sector investment of over $300 billion according to the current Commerce Department.
The U.S. leaned heavily on alliances with Japan, South Korea, Australia, the Philippines, and India (although India is still relying on Russia for energy and defense). These partnerships strengthened deterrence but also exposed a tension: many regional states depend on China economically while relying on American security guarantees.
### **4\. Russia -Ukraine**
Under President Trump, the U.S. has moved from a strategy of backing Ukraine unconditionally to one aimed at pressuring them towards making territorial concessions. That approach has diverged sharply from Europe's position and produced the deepest rift between the United States and NATO since the Cold War.
At the same time, Russia found ways to blunt the impact of European sanctions by tightening its ties with China, Iran, and North Korea and redirecting energy exports eastward. Europe, meanwhile, moved quickly in the opposite direction: rearming at levels not seen in decades (NATO now has a 3.5% GDP to defense spending goal) and completing its break from Russian gas. The result was a permanent reshaping of energy markets--and a clearer sense that Europe was preparing for a longer, more uncertain security future.
### **5\. Iran & Proxy Networks**
In 2025, Iran's proxy network remained one of the most disruptive forces in global security by steadily disrupting trade, energy flows, and military planning.
The clearest example was the Red Sea.
Iran-aligned Houthi forces in Yemen repeatedly attacked commercial shipping, forcing many vessels to reroute around Africa. Transit times lengthened, costs rose sharply, and a major global trade artery remained under constant threat. Shipping costs rose 300% on certain routes with 40% of the world's container traffic rerouted due to threats.

The U.S. and the U.K. responded with sustained naval and air operations, turning maritime security into an ongoing military obligation.
Elsewhere, Iran-backed militias in Iraq and Syria carried out periodic attacks on U.S. forces, raising costs and constraining American flexibility without triggering direct escalation. In Lebanon, Hezbollah remained active enough to keep Israel and the region on edge, even as its capabilities weakened.
The impact extended beyond security. Energy and insurance markets began pricing geopolitical risks as baseline metrics, supply chains adjusted, and defense spending increased across multiple regions.
### **6\. Israel & Palestine**
After two years of war, a major breakthrough finally emerged in the conflict between Israel and Hamas.
In October 2025, Israel and Hamas signed an agreement, implementing the first phase of a U.S.-backed framework that ended large-scale fighting and secured the release of the remaining 20 living Israeli hostages in exchange for Palestinian prisoners and limited Israeli withdrawals.
The United States played the central role, coordinating closely with longstanding Arab partners. Qatar and Egypt served as key intermediaries, while broader regional cooperation made the deal possible.

Militarily, Israel emerged having significantly weakened Hamas and degraded Iran's wider proxy network, including Hezbollah, while disrupting Iranian entrenchment in Syria. But those gains came at a cost. The war reshaped regional alignments, paused Saudi-Israeli normalization, disrupted Red Sea shipping, and left Israel more diplomatically isolated.
What comes next for Gaza and the West Bank is far more complex. Phase two focuses on demilitarization of Hamas, deploying an interim security force, withdrawing Israeli troops to agreed lines, and installing a technocratic Palestinian governing authority. Phase three goes further, envisioning full Israeli withdrawal, a permanent governance transition, large-scale reconstruction, and an internationally backed political horizon for Palestinian self-determination.
### **7\. Critical Minerals**
In 2025, critical minerals moved from a niche supply-chain concern to a core pillar of global power.
Demand for lithium, nickel, cobalt, graphite, and rare earths surged as electric vehicles, AI infrastructure, and advanced weapons scaled faster than supply. At the same time, one reality became impossible to ignore: China controls much of the world's mineral refining and processing capacity. That dominance turned minerals into leverage.
China used export licensing and customs delays to disrupt markets, triggering price volatility and forcing manufacturers to stockpile or delay projects.
The United States and its allies responded by expanding domestic processing, signing supply agreements, and pushing "friendshoring" with partners like Australia, Canada, Japan, and parts of Latin America and Africa. Europe accelerated recycling efforts. India and Japan built stockpiles.
Meanwhile, producing countries raised royalties and demanded more local processing, increasing costs and political risk.
Access now shapes industrial policy, defense readiness, and long-term economic resilience. In a fragmented global economy, whoever controls minerals increasingly controls the future.
### **8\. Fragmentation of the Global Order**
2025 is the year the idea of a shared global order truly began to crack.
Instead of a world organized around common rules and institutions, geopolitics began to operate through overlapping and competing spheres of influence. The United States and European Union diverged on Ukraine strategy. Asian, Middle Eastern, and African states deepened ties with China and Russia without fully breaking from the West. Alliances became more conditional, more transactional, and less automatic.
Multilateral institutions struggled to keep up. The UN Security Council deadlocked repeatedly. The G20 failed to reach consensus. Trade reform stalled. These bodies still exist, but they no longer shape outcomes during crises. Problem-solving shifted toward bilateral deals, regional blocs, and ad hoc coalitions anchored by common self-interest.
The Global South played a central role. Many governments resisted sanctions and emphasized sovereignty, industrial development, and flexibility over alignment. Energy and trade decisions became increasingly political. Supply chains became politically gated, and economic volatility increased as companies navigated conflicting standards and regulatory regimes.
By the end of 2025, geopolitics was no longer defined by the liberal global order shepherded by the United States. Instead, it had become an increasingly multipolar system defined by hedging, fragmentation, and self-interest--conditions that will shape global decision-making well into 2026 and beyond.
### **9\. Reindustrialization of America**
In light of the fragmenting global order, and in response to Chinese economic leverage, 2025 became the year in which the United States aggressively pursued domestic reindustrialization.
What had begun as policy ambition rooted in populism turned into large-scale construction catalyzed by national security needs. Semiconductor fabs, battery plants, grid upgrades, data centers, and defense manufacturing facilities moved from planning to execution. Public incentives combined with private capital and geopolitical pressure to pull production back toward U.S. soil faster than most forecasts anticipated.
The reasons were practical. Supply chains had proven fragile and critical minerals were concentrated abroad. AI-driven electricity demand surged while defense stockpiles needed rebuilding. Together, these pressures pushed the U.S. to treat industrial capacity as a national-security asset, not just an economic one.
Manufacturers expanded domestic chip and battery production, while federal agencies accelerated munitions, rare-earth processing, and telecommunications manufacturing. Grid capacity and permitting became critical impairments, rather than cautious constraints.
The shift reshaped the domestic economy. States competed for factories and workers. Workforce training expanded. Industrial construction reached its highest level in decades.
By year's end, it was clear this was not a temporary political cycle. Reindustrialization had become a durable pillar of U.S. economic and security strategy.
### **10\. A Trump Corollary to the Monroe Doctrine**
In 2025, the Western Hemisphere became a renewed focus of U.S. strategy.
The Trump administration treated expanding Chinese and Russian influence in Latin America as a direct strategic risk due to their control over energy, minerals, and infrastructure.

The underlying message is that strategic footholds near U.S. borders matter again.
The logic explicitly mirrored the Monroe Doctrine, updated for a modern economy. Instead of rival empires, the concern was foreign financing, security cooperation, and control over ports, pipelines, mining zones, and trade routes.
Venezuela sat at the center of this shift. It deepened ties with Beijing and Moscow and expanded state control over mining and energy assets. Washington responded with sanctions and investment scrutiny while working to secure alternative suppliers in Brazil, Chile, and Guyana.
Many Latin American and Caribbean governments resisted choosing sides, pushing instead for diversified partnerships and greater economic autonomy.
***
If 2025 was the year the old rules finally gave way, 2026 will be the year the consequences set in. None of these shifts are finished stories. The alignments, tradeoffs, and power plays now underway will shape markets, security, and politics for the next era. We'll be following what comes next, and what it means, as this new global reality takes shape.
