name: AOC
subject: A radical and outlandish act
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
We must remember in times such as this: we are not the crazy ones.
#
It has always been in our nation’s darkest chapters that our brightest lights emerge.
And we — each and every one of us, whether we like it not — are living history today.
From our nation’s beginning, American democracy has always reflected the struggle between humanity’s highest and lowest natures: between subjects and citizens, the Confederacy and the Union, freedom and bondage, labor and capital rights and regimes.
**It is no different today**.
The struggle of our time today is the single most unjust concentration of wealth in the hands of the very few, unseen in our nation’s and the world’s history.
**It is a time in which demanding what should be considered the most basic tenets of human dignity is considered a radical and outlandish act**.
It is a time such as today, when demanding affordable housing and decent wages is considered a radical and outlandish act.
That we could afford our lives, our groceries, our transit? Is considered a radical and outlandish act.
That we accept our neighbor as ourselves? A radical and outlandish act.
We must remember in times such as this: **we are not the crazy ones**. We are not the outlandish ones.
They want us to think we are crazy — we are sane.
To demand affordable housing, a decent wage, the right to healthcare, that we pay to care for our people instead of the flattening of Palestinians and oppressed people abroad is not a radical act — _it is basic and core humanity_.
We will send a loud message to President Donald Trump: **his authoritarianism is no good here**.
We will send a message to ICE: that **secret police do not belong here**.
When I think about what is happening throughout our nation today, and what Donald Trump has done in his demolition of the White House — we must let him know:
That house doesn’t belong to him. It belongs to us. **It belongs to the people of this country**.
There has been a day before this presidency, and there will be a day after.
**And it belongs to us**.
[Help us continue the fight with a grassroots contribution today.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20260106.Y2i6ew&refcode=em20260106-10529-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=10529%2E2526796%2EzdmyrQ)
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20260106.Y2i6ew&refcode=em20260106-10529-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=10529%2E2526796%2EzdmyrQ)**
Thank you for being a part of this movement.
In solidarity,
Alexandria

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20260106?refcode=em20260106-10529-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=21&refcode2=10529_2526796_zdmyrQ&akid=10529%2E2526796%2EzdmyrQ)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20260106-10529-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=22&akid=10529%2E2526796%2EzdmyrQ)**
---|---
[](https://www.facebook.com/OcasioCortez/?t=23&akid=10529%2E2526796%2EzdmyrQ) | [](https://twitter.com/AOC?t=24&akid=10529%2E2526796%2EzdmyrQ) | [](https://www.instagram.com/aoc/?t=25&akid=10529%2E2526796%2EzdmyrQ) | [](https://bsky.app/profile/aoc.bsky.social?t=26&akid=10529%2E2526796%2EzdmyrQ)
---|---|---|---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.

name: Adam Schiff
subject: A day I won’t forget
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
|
# [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/61?t=1&akid=9372%2E5257348%2EG40_rm)
Derek,
Few days in our country’s history have made as big an impact on me, and on our democracy, as January 6, 2021.
_Even five years removed, I remember the January 6th attack on the Capitol like it was yesterday._
The sounds of the mob outside. Of insurrectionists storming the Capitol building and breaking doors and windows to get in. Of the urgent discussions between members of Congress deciding how to protect ourselves.
The sight of Capitol police officers running toward danger. Of my colleagues crouched on the floor, clutching masks to protect themselves from tear gas. Of thousands of protestors assaulting Capitol Police and desecrating the hallowed halls of Congress.
I remember evacuating the House Floor — where I was a manager of the debate over certification of the election — all while rioters were beating down the doors. I turned to someone I didn’t recognize who had grabbed a large wooden post to protect himself, and asked him how long he’d been serving. It was his first week. I remember saying to him, for lack of a better response, “it’s not always like this.”
The fear and the urgency were palpable, as was the anger over what this mob was doing to overturn a free and fair election on behalf of Donald Trump. And then, finally, once the fighting was over and we had fulfilled our duty to certify the election, the recognition that our democracy had survived, but barely.
Derek, I will remember that day for a long time. But I will also remember what my GOP colleagues in Congress did after just as much.
After the rioting and the mayhem, a majority of Republican members of Congress still voted to overturn the election. Still. 147 members of Congress — most of whom are still in office today. Insurrectionists, too. Albeit in suits and ties.
Since that day, we have seen continued threats to our Constitution and our democracy growing and metascizing. Trump’s first act of his second term was to pardon all of these violent offenders, people who beat police officers, gauged them and sprayed them with bear spray.
Our democracy was battered and bruised on January 6, 2021, and it has not yet recovered.
**And Derek, it’s not lost on me that, as a U.S. Senator, I voted to certify the 2024 election results of the same man who incited the January 6 insurrection. I had to fulfill that duty, because it was the will of the American people, and I take this responsibility seriously.**
January 6 will forever mark a somber day in our country’s history. The impacts of that horrific day still reverberate through the halls of Congress. But we will get through this difficult time in our history. America is a deeply resilient country filled with patriotic citizens who love our democracy, and those who do, vastly outnumber those trying to tear it down.
Please take care of yourselves, and thank you for all your support during this pivotal time in our history.
— Adam
[](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q12026_abs_em.43HrbP&refcode=AU_ABS_CL_GEN_GEN_AC90_20260106_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=4&akid=9372%2E5257348%2EG40_rm)

name: Costello for U.S. Senate
subject: Help put David on the Democratic primary ballot
body:
Signature collection is underway—join us in qualifying David for the June primary.

| | | | | [|
---](https://costelloforsenate.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60d37adbc910a9a40e00b14f5&id=16f39a4520&e=5d7176e969)
| | | | | | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://costelloforsenate.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60d37adbc910a9a40e00b14f5&id=c3269e2951&e=5d7176e969)
| |
Friends,
We’ve begun collecting signatures for Democratic primary ballot access and we’re seeking your help. The signature collection process is straightforward. We provide you with blank 8.5-inch by 14-inch petition forms and ask that you ask your friends and neighbors who are registered Democrats if they’d be willing to sign the petition to simply put David’s name on Maine’s June Democratic primary ballot. We need to collect 2,000 signatures by March 16, 2026, to qualify. There are any number of ways and places to collect signatures, and we can provide that information to you. By way of example, David’s grandmother collected more than 200 signatures (in 2001) by simply keeping a petition form in her purse and asking friends to sign the petition as she went about her daily activities. If you are interested in helping, please let us know by emailing us at [campaign@costelloforsenate.com](mailto:campaign@costelloforsenate.com).
Thank you and best,Team Costello
P.S. For those interested in David’s thoughts on the Trump Administration foreign affairs, please see David’s recent Facebook posts below...
1/6/26Senior presidential aide Stephen Miller and President Trump are wrong. There is no justification for American imperialism (i.e., the extension of America’s power and influence in the world through coercion or force), nor imperialism exercised by any other nation. The world’s colonial powers realized this more than a century ago. US Government actions that run counter to American and international law and ideals, including the Universal Declaration of Human Rights, will only weaken our nation’s long-term economic and physical security. It’s absurd and dangerous to think the United States, or any country, Russia and China included, has the right to forcefully take over any other territory or country (including Greenland, Taiwan, or Ukraine).

Senator Susan Collins and her colleagues need to reign in the mis-guided imperial aims of the Trump Administration before they further damage our country’s standing in the world. If the Administration truly wants to bolster our long-term economic and physical security it should strengthen, not continue to weaken, America’s foreign policy and foreign aid agencies. Boost our international competitiveness by increasing, not decreasing, investment in transportation, energy, and communications infrastructure, and scientific research and development. Negotiate smart, fair, and comprehensive trade agreements that include a global minimum corporate tax and enhanced labor and environmental protections, not rely on shortsighted, debilitating trade tariffs. And prioritize the enactment of far-reaching international nuclear and conventional arms control agreements. Only by becoming more fully, intelligently, and responsibly engaged in world affairs, not less so, is the USA likely to become more secure and prosperous.
1/5/26My FB comments yesterday regarding Venezuela were not meant as an endorsement of what the Trump Administration has done (or is doing) in Venezuela, but largely a criticism in the form of a couple of brief statements (including one that was anti-Maduro) and questions regarding where we go from here... I 100% support the establishment of a legitimate, democratically elected government in Venezuela; not a puppet regime largely designed to enrich petroleum interests in Venezuela, the USA, and elsewhere (see yesterday’s comments). Having worked promoting peace and democracy in conflict/post conflict countries (e.g., Cambodia, Haiti, Bosnia, Kosovo and Serbia), I’ve seen first-hand the gut-wrenching harm and oppression doled out by ruthless dictators and war lords, and I know how difficult it is to establish genuine democracies in which the citizenry is free, politically equal, and prosperous...
Moreover, I understand how imperfect our own democracy is, and that is why, in large part, I am running for the US Senate to substantially strengthen our weak checks and balances system and better ensure that no president or presidential administration acts illegally (either domestically or abroad)...
For more information on David’s background, experience, and policy positions, visit our Campaign’s website ([costelloforsenate.com.](https://costelloforsenate.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60d37adbc910a9a40e00b14f5&id=0367c7a159&e=5d7176e969)) and David’s Facebook page ([https://www.facebook.com/CostelloForMaine](https://costelloforsenate.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60d37adbc910a9a40e00b14f5&id=524158552a&e=5d7176e969)).

Team,

Now that we're officially in an election year, we're kicking our
signature-gathering into high gear. Even though it’s January, we’re
thinking about April, when we have to turn in our petition signatures to
get on the primary ballot.

That means planning our budget and hiring organizing fellows to help us
gather petition signatures. Ground game matters, and building up this
infrastructure now will pay dividends not only in signature gathering, but
all the way through the general election.

January 6th, 2021. We all remember what happened five years ago today. As Congress met to count the electoral votes of the 2020 election, Donald Trump sent his supporters to storm the Capitol. The images of that day are forever seared in our memories. That in a country based on freedom and democracy, the current president once encouraged people to attack our great nation's Capitol. Things have only gotten worse since he’s retaken office. In this past year, Donald Trump has been a bigger threat to the United States democracy than this country has ever seen. Our own president tramples the Constitution and threatens democracy each and every day. I know how precious our democracy is and how this moment requires us to defend our nation. My parents fled a dictatorship in Cuba to give our family the gift of growing up in a country of freedom. **But our democracy is failing.** [**Team, it’s more important than ever that we pledge to support candidates who will protect our sacred democracy. Please, sign my pledge to vote for pro-democracy candidates down the ballot this year.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122801500/593500273/-266784345?source=LOC_EM_PET_2026.01.06_Pledge_B1_F1_S1_C1_AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYwODgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2U5MzA1OGUtNDRlYi1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ALJjMExZPEWpUv53O26JO1yFhWrl7EqOyCZBs0Sef1s=&emci=d435495e-36eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=7e93058e-44eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=626985) | | [ SIGN THE PLEDGE ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122801501/593500274/-266784345?source=LOC_EM_PET_2026.01.06_Pledge_B1_F1_S1_C1_AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYwODgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2U5MzA1OGUtNDRlYi1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ALJjMExZPEWpUv53O26JO1yFhWrl7EqOyCZBs0Sef1s=&emci=d435495e-36eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=7e93058e-44eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=626985)

name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: AIPAC endorsed 109 insurrectionists.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Today marks the fifth anniversary of the deadly insurrection at the U.S.
Capitol — and the MAGA cult is now trying to rewrite history with Donald
Trump back in the White House.

Trump has since pardoned all of the Jan. 6th insurrectionists who
terrorized the Capitol and fired more than a dozen Department of Justice
officials who prosecuted them. He also appointed multiple election deniers
to positions of power in his administration.

Who else backed pro-insurrection Republicans even after they voted to
overturn the 2020 election? AIPAC.

AIPAC endorsed 109 of the 147 Republicans who voted to overturn the 2020
election results in the 2022 midterms.

It wasn’t enough for them to buy our politicians and elections — they
wanted to prove just how devoted they are to destroying what’s left of our
democracy.

No Democrat should accept money from this GOP megadonor-funded group that
supports insurrection and genocide.

That’s why Justice Democrats has endorsed a slate of progressive,
working-class candidates for Congress in 2026 who are running to unseat
AIPAC-backed Democratic incumbents.

Our first primary elections are less than two months away, so we need as
many grassroots resources as possible to get out the vote for our
anti-AIPAC candidates who will boldly stand up to Trump and MAGA
Republicans’ authoritarianism.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260106.6e4aee&refcode=em20260106-37346&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1002&akid=37346%2E1203867%2EQw_3K1 ]Chip in $5 or whatever you can today to help us elect
more progressives to Congress this year and defeat billionaire-backed
far-right extremism!

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20260106.6e4aee&refcode=em20260106-37346&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1021&akid=37346%2E1203867%2EQw_3K1 ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20260106-37346-footer&t=1022&refcode2=37346_1203867_Qw_3K1&akid=37346%2E1203867%2EQw_3K1

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. name: Sharif Street
subject: This is personal
disclaimer_text: Paid for By Sharif Street for Us
body:
A lifelong Philadelphian, public servant, and fighter for justice. Join Sharif as he brings bold, thoughtful leadership to Washington. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/RNlM66BHF0WVUQpwM4Eu2g2?unsubscribedata=6dSA1eSN1KmZCEa8RKxXDROYWfJzogQzGu3Tx0TVjYYuPyBASPRXppjizQIp8VOA1n4bq1oNcGG4UC7%2Bf%2BOYMNjZyV6I8QeI4%2FQZmrXbFa0CUBCK8F8SWo81GdUx69AHTw%2Fe%2BgqEc9MTV2ciZ5NFrUkWfWQe8%2BZCgq0CEo6kzHyUmhpd00ES%2FHUXIPj0cclNCW%2FE52KFWX%2FDKmo%2FGYqS%2Blt%2BQspnKacUKVCUbBIMvFg99nUYej6tKWN1AfmI1oVpHMQTp5cE9BXyRws8u%2FqKUXe5rqAZ%2Ffz5DdH57053Kdv9y3vxYttCtMW2J9xhjatErLvIPlt1B8o5ojdrbn%2FPGeKSxXxQGTPEumkeFnU6T0Wua3ignQ8yajYrees9g5N966vGqgHdIw7EPyUN%2FFhZOdYjlw%2B6XYD0Y3X3KpDkOv90fTMcOeCKL2Is1sOXSUI4)
---
| |
---
Peter, on January 6th, 2021, a violent mob stormed the United States Capitol to overturn a free and fair election. Capitol Police officers were attacked, the building was breached, and the peaceful transfer of power was threatened.

For me, this moment is personal.

When Donald Trump tried to throw out Pennsylvanians’ votes in 2020, I didn’t stay silent. I took him to court a _dozen times_ — and won every single case. I faced thousands of threats because of this, but stayed the course. No politician gets to decide whose votes count, and no one is above the law.

January 6th wasn’t an isolated incident. It was the result of lies meant to undermine our democracy. Five years later, accountability still matters. The truth still matters. And it matters deeply that we do not excuse, minimize, or rewrite what happened that day.

I’ve spent my career fighting for accountability and the rule of law, especially when our democracy is under attack. Pardoning those who participated in the January 6 violence weakens the foundations of our democracy.

[If you agree that the January 6th insurrectionists should be held accountable — and that our democracy is worth protecting — add your name to stand up for the rule of law.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122801769/593501139/-856054772?source=EM_NBI_SFU_20260106_FR_Jan6_0_1MO_sharifstreet_NA_NA_Jan6&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84MzEwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhY2NkNGNjNy00NWViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xRcWxvsLod37nOICM4cyBqP043ReUtX-EB2MZHJvC4M=&emci=12b6f931-41eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=accd4cc7-45eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=15053672) | **[ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122801771/593501141/-856054772?source=EM_NBI_SFU_20260106_FR_Jan6_0_1MO_sharifstreet_NA_NA_Jan6&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84MzEwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhY2NkNGNjNy00NWViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xRcWxvsLod37nOICM4cyBqP043ReUtX-EB2MZHJvC4M=&emci=12b6f931-41eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=accd4cc7-45eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=15053672) **

Thank you for standing with me and for believing in a democracy that works for everyone.

In solidarity,

Sharif Street

I know this will be a shock, but I need you to hear it all the same. |

name: Tobias Read
subject: Response Requested: Ban phones in public schools?
body:
Your input is important to me| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122801846/593501479/-569431239?refcode=260106em&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzI2MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZjJhYzk3NS00NmViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=x50TkmIwjMoPPVcuF3pTIUaQMRCe87jwhriuyfXbFJ0=)
---
| | As Oregon’s Secretary of State and a member of the State Board of Education, I take our state’s K-12 education system extremely seriously. As parents and educators become increasingly aware of the impact of social media and cell phone use on students, conversations about the potential need to ban cell phones in schools have gained momentum. **That’s why I’m reaching out today. I wanted to get your input on this critical question about the future of education and raising our kids. Your input is important to me.** [**So let me know: Do YOU support banning cell phones in public schools?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122801847/593501480/-577767784?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzI2MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZjJhYzk3NS00NmViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=x50TkmIwjMoPPVcuF3pTIUaQMRCe87jwhriuyfXbFJ0=&emci=edf7b279-24eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=6f2ac975-46eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=30662126)
---
[ **ANSWER NOW** ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122801848/593501481/-577767784?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzI2MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZjJhYzk3NS00NmViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=x50TkmIwjMoPPVcuF3pTIUaQMRCe87jwhriuyfXbFJ0=&emci=edf7b279-24eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=6f2ac975-46eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=30662126)
---
The work to build a world-class education system for Oregon’s kids is never done. There will always be more for us to do when it comes to building a public school system Oregon can be proud of — and I’m ready to dig in. **But with such divisive questions facing our state over the next few years, I wanted to solicit your input on some of the most pressing issues.** **[Please spare a few seconds to let me know where you stand on cell phones in the classroom. Do you support banning them from our schools?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122801849/593501482/-577767784?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzI2MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZjJhYzk3NS00NmViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=x50TkmIwjMoPPVcuF3pTIUaQMRCe87jwhriuyfXbFJ0=&emci=edf7b279-24eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=6f2ac975-46eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=30662126)[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122801850/593501483/-577767784?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NzI2MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ZjJhYzk3NS00NmViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=x50TkmIwjMoPPVcuF3pTIUaQMRCe87jwhriuyfXbFJ0=&emci=edf7b279-24eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=6f2ac975-46eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=30662126)**
---
Thank you, Tobias Read
Oregon Secretary of State

body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=40668%2E3422442%2ECgPcP6)
BREAKING NEWS: Katie’s billionaire opponent has now spent over $16 MILLION on TV ads. **[Can you rush a $5 donation to help us keep up?](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260106_1_ak.LREdOc&refcode=em20260106-40668&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=40668%2E3422442%2ECgPcP6)**
Peter,
I’ll be blunt: this isn’t the email I wanted to send you today.
We just found out that **my billionaire opponent is dumping more money into this race—spending over $16 MILLION to blanket California in ads.**
To become California’s next governor, we need to get our message in front of as many people as possible. I’m traveling the state and meeting voters to do just that, **but we need your financial support to stay competitive with a billionaire trying to buy this election.**
When a billionaire jumped into this race, we knew days like this would come. It’s going to be a test for our people powered movement, but I know we can pass it.
**[Peter, can you chip in $5 to help us get our message in front of voters? Thousands of grassroots donations can add up to beat anything a billionaire throws our way.](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21531?c0=em_ms_kp_20260106_1_ak.LREdOc&refcode=em20260106-40668&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=4&akid=40668%2E3422442%2ECgPcP6)**

Katie Porter

Katie is running to be California’s next governor because California and our country need a leader who will stand up to Trump to lower costs, protect our rights, and combat climate change. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4g-em-footer?refcode=em20260106-40668&amounts=&t=24&refcode2=40668_3422442_CgPcP6&akid=40668%2E3422442%2ECgPcP6)**

body:

An evening of conversation, community, and support for the campaign | [View in browser](https://gxwexbq.clicks.mlsend.com/td/c/eyJ2Ijoie1wiYVwiOjE2NjExMzIsXCJsXCI6MTc1ODgzNzc5OTUxMjk3NzQxLFwiclwiOjE3NTg4Mzc5MTIxNTEwMTA3OX0iLCJzIjoiMzI5ZmM0OGM4YmJlYzM0NCJ9)

# YOU'RE INVITED!
|
---
Dear , I hope this note finds you well. You are warmly invited to join us for a small, private **house party fundraiser** in support of Dax Alexander—an evening of meaningful conversation, good food, and community. This intimate gathering will bring together a limited group of supporters (15–20 guests) for a relaxed, personal discussion about the campaign, the road ahead, and the values that guide our work. **Event Details**
Location: Smith County (address provided following ticket purchase)
Date: Friday, January 16, 2026
Time: 7:00 PM to 9:00 PM
Attire: Casual **Suggested Contribution Levels**
* **Supporter ($50):** Admission to the event and campaign thank-you
* **Champion ($100-250):** Reserved seating and post-event recognition
* **Leader ($500-$750):** Recognition on event materials and an invitation to the happy hour prior to the event
* **Visionary ($1,000):** _Top tier_ — name listed on the event invitation and special recognition during the evening, along with dedicated one-on-one time with Dax after the event

Space is intentionally limited to keep the evening personal, so we encourage you to RSVP early. We would be honored to have you join us for this special night of connection and conversation. If you have any questions or would like to confirm your attendance, please reach out to Team Dax at [vote@daxforcongress.com](mailto:vote@daxforcongress.com) or text us at **903-284-5893**. Warm regards,
Team Dax
|
---
|
---
|
---
|
##### Dax Alexander For Congress
5380 Old Bullard Rd. Ste. 600-178, Tyler
United States of America |

name: Team Kinter
subject: I watched January 6th from Deployment
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MARIALANA KINTER FOR CONGRESS
body:
Now I'm challenging a Congressman that defended it.

| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://kinterforcongress.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f6a723b5c3bc1add1f7a8ca4d&id=471f22c1e0&e=d995135712)
[|
---](https://kinterforcongress.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f6a723b5c3bc1add1f7a8ca4d&id=c9aae9f7c0&e=d995135712)
| |

**I watched the January 6th insurrection while deployed in the South China Sea.**
Communications were shut down on and off the ship. No internet.
What we knew came from a single news network playing on ship TV.
From halfway around the world, in uniform, I watched an attack on our democracy.
I didn’t understand how a national leader could call for an attack on our nation.
**I will never understand it.**
What I do understand is this:
The greatest threat to our democracy is right here at home, and authority without accountability is dangerous.
Defending democracy does not end overseas, and it does not end when the uniform comes off.
**We need accountability in office. We need leaders in Congress.**
**That's why I'm running for Congress in FL-7th District.**

---
|
---
| |

**Pitch in to help us bring accountability back to Congress and defend our democracy here at home**

---
| [DONATE NOW!](https://kinterforcongress.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=f6a723b5c3bc1add1f7a8ca4d&id=0fc2f71a97&e=d995135712)

Let's show them what real service looks like,
#### **
Marialana Kinter**

clean_body: Now I'm challenging a Congressman that defended it. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | I watched the January 6th insurrection while deployed in the South China Sea. Communications were shut down on and off the ship. No internet. What we knew came from a single news network playing on ship TV. From halfway around the world, in uniform, I watched an attack on our democracy. I didn’t understand how a national leader could call for an attack on our nation. I will never understand it. What I do understand is this: The greatest threat to our democracy is right here at home, and authority without accountability is dangerous. Defending democracy does not end overseas, and it does not end when the uniform comes off. We need accountability in office. We need leaders in Congress. That's why I'm running for Congress in FL-7th District. | | | Pitch in to help us bring accountability back to Congress and defend our democracy here at home | | | Let's show them what real service looks like, Marialana Kinter Navy Veteran, Nuclear Reactor Supervisor Candidate for Congress, District 7 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Restoring Trust, Rebuilding Our future. | | | | | | | Marialana Kinter is a former member of the U.S. Navy. Use of her military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense. | | | | | | | | | | Marialana Kinter For Congress PO Box 620511 Oviedo, FL 32762-0511 * USA or FOR BY MARIALANA KINTER FOR CONGRESS | | |

Peter,
January 6, 2021 wasn’t the start of Trump’s attacks on our democracy, and as this past year showed us, it definitely wasn’t his last.
Regardless of race, age, gender, faith, preferred language, or ZIP Code, all who call our country home deserve fair representation from elected leaders who want to preserve our democracy, not destroy it.
We won’t stand idly by as this administration and Texas Republicans CONTINUE to gaslight us and minimize the clear-cut violence we witnessed on January 6. In our state alone, more than 100 Texans were directly involved in the attack on January 6 [https://www.texastribune.org/2025/01/21/texans-insurrection-capitol-trump-pardon/] , making us the state with the second most insurrectionists that day.
DONATE NOW to help us reach as many voters across Texas as possible to counter MAGA in 2026 and beyond! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/texas-organizing-project-political-action-committee-3?refcode=TexasJan6&amount=15.00&refcode2=55ea9719-4beb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa]

There’s not a doubt in our minds that Trump will wield every bit of his power in 2026 to further twist our government to enrich himself, weaponize laws to attack communities, and stifle opposition to his extremist agenda.
Here at TOP PAC, we’ll be electorally fighting back at every opportunity, but we can’t do the work we do without the support of people like you.

clean_body: [] Peter, January 6, 2021 wasn’t the start of Trump’s attacks on our democracy, and as this past year showed us, it definitely wasn’t his last. Regardless of race, age, gender, faith, preferred language, or ZIP Code, all who call our country home deserve fair representation from elected leaders who want to preserve our democracy, not destroy it. We won’t stand idly by as this administration and Texas Republicans CONTINUE to gaslight us and minimize the clear-cut violence we witnessed on January 6. In our state alone, more than 100 Texans were directly involved in the attack on January 6 [] , making us the state with the second most insurrectionists that day. DONATE NOW to help us reach as many voters across Texas as possible to counter MAGA in 2026 and beyond! [] [] Texas is ground zero in the fight to defend democracy. From our governor to our senators, representatives, and local officials, TOO MANY Texas Republicans to this day remain complicit with what transpired on January 6. There’s not a doubt in our minds that Trump will wield every bit of his power in 2026 to further twist our government to enrich himself, weaponize laws to attack communities, and stifle opposition to his extremist agenda. Here at TOP PAC, we’ll be electorally fighting back at every opportunity, but we can’t do the work we do without the support of people like you. PITCH IN $5, $15, $25, or any amount you can to help us build progressive power in Texas! [] Let's get to work. – Texas Organizing Project PAC [] Texas Organizing Project PAC P.O. Peter,
Our opponent just scored an endorsement from DMFI, Democratic Majority for Israel.

Because apparently, nothing says “listening to St. Louis families” like taking guidance from a billionaire-funded lobbying machine.

AIPAC and its allies are preparing to flood our opponent’s campaign with millions of dollars in outside, extremist money. In fact, groups tied to AIPAC already make up the majority of his fundraising, and we know they’ll spend whatever it takes to try to buy this seat.
That means we need the resources right now to fight back, stay on the air, organize voters, and make sure people aren’t drowned out by billionaire-funded interests.

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

But almost no one knows who’s keeping the king alive.

NVIDIA’s new Blackwell chips depend on a tiny company with **7,000 patents** \- technology so critical that the U.S. government blocked NVIDIA from acquiring it, calling its innovations **“vital to national security.”**

And the timing couldn’t be more explosive:

The U.S. and China are locked in an **AI war** \- Washington is restricting chip exports, China is limiting rare-earth metals, and AI chips have become the new “oil.”

And while retail investors chase the obvious play... billionaires like **Ken Griffin** and **Stan Druckenmiller** are quietly buying the one company NVIDIA _cannot_ replace - its **“Secret Supplier.”**

On **January 19th at 5 PM** , NVIDIA is preparing a major Blackwell announcement.

clean_body: Everyone knows NVIDIA is the king of AI. But almost no one knows who’s keeping the king alive. NVIDIA’s new Blackwell chips depend on a tiny company with 7,000 patents - technology so critical that the U.S. government blocked NVIDIA from acquiring it, calling its innovations “vital to national security.”| | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | Everyone knows NVIDIA is the king of AI. But almost no one knows who’s keeping the king alive. NVIDIA’s new Blackwell chips depend on a tiny company with 7,000 patents \- technology so critical that the U.S. government blocked NVIDIA from acquiring it, calling its innovations “vital to national security.” And the timing couldn’t be more explosive: The U.S. and China are locked in an AI war \- Washington is restricting chip exports, China is limiting rare-earth metals, and AI chips have become the new “oil.” And while retail investors chase the obvious play... billionaires like Ken Griffin and Stan Druckenmiller are quietly buying the one company NVIDIA cannot replace - its “Secret Supplier.” On January 19th at 5 PM , NVIDIA is preparing a major Blackwell announcement. clean_body: | | | | | | | Peter, 2026—we're here. The democrats have been lying in wait. 2026 is their chance to retake the House. 2026 is their chance to retake the Senate. And 2026 is the year to stop them in their tracks. Have they learned any lessons since 2024? Probably not. Will this be the year they focus on middle America instead of the rich, coastal elites? No. But this is shaping up to be the year they raise and spend more than they ever have. They want the America-First movement ended once and for all. Peter, if you want to do something about it, you can. You can join me as I fight back the radical democrat agenda in the Senate. You can join the movement of grassroots patriots who alone may be powerless, but united, can't be stopped! So, do you want to do something about it, or stay on the sidelines? | | In Liberty, Senator Josh Hawley Save this contact card **. Fellow Conservative,

Congress is like a battlefield—thrilling, mission-driven, and full of conflict. At least no one's shooting at us here. Well, not with guns, anyway.

The out-of-touch fake news "reporters" at Politico decided they would target the most dangerous men in DC: **Navy SEALs.**

We all ran for office because we were still hungry to protect and fight for our country. The war may be over for us, but we can't abandon the part of our lives that made us who we are. We were part of an elite unit on the front lines, and Congress is somewhere else we can be out front doing something special for our country.

_**Yet the liberal news corporations, who _desperately_ want to keep the status quo, _attacked us_ for wanting to take on the Swamp establishment and business-as-usual going on in our government.**_

All of us –**Navy SEALs Tim Sheehy, Eli Crane, and Ryan Zinke** – are teaming up to stop career politicians and lazy bureaucrats, but we're up against more than just the DC establishment. We're up against the liberal media, RINOs, and revenge-seeking Democrats.

clean_body: They’re targeting the most dangerous men in DC: Navy SEALs. | Fellow Conservative, Congress is like a battlefield—thrilling, mission-driven, and full of conflict. At least no one's shooting at us here. Well, not with guns, anyway. The out-of-touch fake news "reporters" at Politico decided they would target the most dangerous men in DC: Navy SEALs. We all ran for office because we were still hungry to protect and fight for our country. The war may be over for us, but we can't abandon the part of our lives that made us who we are. We were part of an elite unit on the front lines, and Congress is somewhere else we can be out front doing something special for our country. Yet the liberal news corporations, who desperately want to keep the status quo, attacked us for wanting to take on the Swamp establishment and business-as-usual going on in our government. All of us –Navy SEALs Tim Sheehy, Eli Crane, and Ryan Zinke – are teaming up to stop career politicians and lazy bureaucrats, but we're up against more than just the DC establishment. We're up against the liberal media, RINOs, and revenge-seeking Democrats. We need you, Fellow Conservative to keep us on the frontlines, bucking the corrupt status quo! -> If the media wants to attack us for having a "warrior mentality" - the mentality to do whatever it takes to achieve a mission... -> If the media wants to attack us for supporting President Trump's MAGA movement - the movement that says we can make America great... -> If the media wants to attack us for taking on the government corruption, fraud, and waste - which has been exposed by Trump's DOGE department... ...Then they don't understand WHY Donald J. | Peter,
MAGA is in love with the ideals of the slave-owning Confederacy, before the messiness of equal rights became a focus for America.
They glorify the Confederacy so much that at Trump's direction, the people who waged war against the United States in 1861 are being honored anew as heroes worthy of our adulation. Pete Hegseth is renaming military bases after Confederate generals, directly violating a 2021 law that banned the practice. And Trump is having Confederate monuments reinstalled across the country.
But things get truly ridiculous when MAGA attempts to lay claim to the principles of the Revolution AND the Confederacy.
Five years ago today, on the morning of January 6, 2021, Boebert declared the start of a Second American Revolution (the same was said by Confederates when they declared war on the Union):

MAGA is in reality more like a Confederacy of Dunces. They are extremely dangerous.
These historical fantasies and macho fever dreams of glory led thousands to the steps of the Capitol -- who then, at the direction of Donald Trump and with weeks of careful planning by conspirators like Lauren Boebert, launched the most violent coup attempt since the Civil War.
The next time Trump or Boebert call for disrupting an election or throwing out the results of one, he and his goons will be fully in charge of the federal government.
We have to act now to defeat Boebert by indisputable margins.
Boebert barely squeaked out her re-election, winning by only 546 votes. When Democrat Trisha Calvarese ran against her in 2024, she had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in the district--outperforming Kamala Harris by 6 points in a red wave election.

clean_body: MAGA lunatics like Boebert may be stupid, but they are still very dangerous. And the next time Trump calls for disrupting an election, he and his goons will be fully in charge of the federal governmen | | | | Trisha Calvarese is the Democrat taking on Lauren Boebert in Colorado's 4th congressional district. Thank you for your crucial support for Trisha. However you can do so at the link at the bottom of this email. | | == | Peter, MAGA is in love with the ideals of the slave-owning Confederacy, before the messiness of equal rights became a focus for America. They glorify the Confederacy so much that at Trump's direction, the people who waged war against the United States in 1861 are being honored anew as heroes worthy of our adulation. Pete Hegseth is renaming military bases after Confederate generals, directly violating a 2021 law that banned the practice. And Trump is having Confederate monuments reinstalled across the country. But things get truly ridiculous when MAGA attempts to lay claim to the principles of the Revolution AND the Confederacy. Five years ago today, on the morning of January 6, 2021, Boebert declared the start of a Second American Revolution (the same was said by Confederates when they declared war on the Union): | == MAGA is in reality more like a Confederacy of Dunces. They are extremely dangerous. These historical fantasies and macho fever dreams of glory led thousands to the steps of the Capitol -- who then, at the direction of Donald Trump and with weeks of careful planning by conspirators like Lauren Boebert, launched the most violent coup attempt since the Civil War. The next time Trump or Boebert call for disrupting an election or throwing out the results of one, he and his goons will be fully in charge of the federal government. We have to act now to defeat Boebert by indisputable margins. Boebert barely squeaked out her re-election, winning by only 546 votes. When Democrat Trisha Calvarese ran against her in 2024, she had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in the district--outperforming Kamala Harris by 6 points in a red wave election. == If you've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly: | | | == | == | == | | | == | == | == | == | Thank you, -- Team Trisha Calvarese | | | == Mail checks to: Trisha 4 CO 9609 S University Blvd., PO Box 631322 Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado | | | | 9609 S University Blvd, | Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 US | |

name: Mark Kelly
subject: They’re not going to shut me up

body:
You swear an oath to the Constitution when you join the military — including the First Amendment right to free speech. We swore to defend the foundational principles of this country. I took that oath seriously 40 years ago, and I still do.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122803608/593508312/-1912233044?refcode=BLU_EM_HSC_2026-01-06_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNjQ4N2UwOS00ZWViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=89R4yOnmVCTjrgz7JFt4SYbVkUBbc8fYr305Bb2ttcM=)

Team:

Pete Hegseth decided he's going to censure me, and is threatening to demote me and reduce my pension.

I got shot at in service to this country. I missed holidays and birthdays. I commanded a space shuttle mission and launched to the International Space Station while my wife recovered from a gunshot wound to the head.

My rank and retirement are things that I earned through my service and sacrifice for this country. Generations of servicemembers have done the same. Pete Hegseth wants to send a message to every single one that he will come after them if they say something he doesn’t like.

You swear an oath to the Constitution when you join the military — including the First Amendment right to free speech. We swore to defend the foundational principles of this country. I took that oath seriously 40 years ago, and I still do.

Pete Hegseth can threaten to reduce my rank and my pension. But I am not going to let him intimidate me, or anyone else. He is not going to stop me from defending our democracy from a president who is hellbent on destroying it.

If they think this is going to shut me up, they’ve got another thing coming.

[_**And if you’re still with me, please: Split a donation between Hillary Scholten and me so we can fight back.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122803609/593508313/-1912233044?refcode=BLU_EM_HSC_2026-01-06_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNjQ4N2UwOS00ZWViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=89R4yOnmVCTjrgz7JFt4SYbVkUBbc8fYr305Bb2ttcM=)

My name is Raja Krishnamoorthi, and I’m running for US Senate.

When times became tough, my parents moved to the United States when I was a baby in hope of the American Dream.

They utilized public housing and SNAP assistance to help our family get by and move us into the middle class.

And now, Donald Trump is gutting that assistance as well as critical programs like Social Security and Medicaid.

Peter, Trump’s first year back in office has been marked by chaos, confusion, and destruction.

**So I REFUSE to sit by idly while he ravages our Democracy and makes life harder for everyday Americans.**

[**_That’s why I’m running for US Senate. To stand up to bullies like Donald Trump and fight for everyday Americans. But I need your support today. Peter, during my time in Congress, I’ve been no stranger to standing up for what’s right.

As part of the House Oversight Committee, I’ve been a part of critical efforts to hold Trump and his MAGA GOP accountable.

I even voted to impeach Donald Trump TWICE because I believe in the power of our Democracy.

And now, we’re at a turning point in our Democracy and we need fighters like me in the Senate now more than ever before.

**As a first-generation American with a funny name, I’ve always felt like I’m on the outside in Washington. **

That’s why I’m more determined than ever to be a voice in the Senate fighting for all of the outsiders and vulnerable Americans Trump continuously attacks.

[**_My election is just 70 days away, Peter. So please, if you’ve read this far and you’re still with me, can I please ask you to rush a small donation to power this campaign to victory >>_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122803654/593508416/-2026962184?amount=10&amounts=250,100,50,25,10,5&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2026.01.06_Launch-V2_B2_F1_S1_C1_30CL-365DON_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzE2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhZTYzYjFkZi00ZGViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=XGCbQi71c7gxCQ4SLl6e1WyQ1bzxW16gTSBTXSI-uyE=)

If you'd prefer, you can . = Hi Peter, My name is Raja Krishnamoorthi, and I’m running for US Senate. When times became tough, my parents moved to the United States when I was a baby in hope of the American Dream. They utilized public housing and SNAP assistance to help our family get by and move us into the middle class. And now, Donald Trump is gutting that assistance as well as critical programs like Social Security and Medicaid. Peter, Trump’s first year back in office has been marked by chaos, confusion, and destruction. So I REFUSE to sit by idly while he ravages our Democracy and makes life harder for everyday Americans. = | | = | = | = | = | = | = Peter, during my time in Congress, I’ve been no stranger to standing up for what’s right. As part of the House Oversight Committee, I’ve been a part of critical efforts to hold Trump and his MAGA GOP accountable. I even voted to impeach Donald Trump TWICE because I believe in the power of our Democracy. And now, we’re at a turning point in our Democracy and we need fighters like me in the Senate now more than ever before. As a first-generation American with a funny name, I’ve always felt like I’m on the outside in Washington. That’s why I’m more determined than ever to be a voice in the Senate fighting for all of the outsiders and vulnerable Americans Trump continuously attacks. = | = | = | = | = | = | = Your support is immensely appreciated. Thank you, Raja | Democrat Raja Krishnamoorthi is running for Senate in Illinois to fight back against Donald Trump and the far-right MAGA movement, protect critical programs like Social Security and Medicare, and rebuild the middle class. But Republicans will spend MILLIONS to flip this seat red. We need every single person reading this email to do their part to fight back against Trump and flip the Senate Majority BLUE. = | | = | = These emails are the best way for our campaign to stay in touch with supporters like you. If you'd prefer to receive fewer emails, click here. If you'd prefer, you can . If you'd like to donate, =. Raja for IL PO Box 681202 Schaumburg, IL 60168 United States | mailto:info@rajaforil.com | PAID FOR BY RAJA FOR ILLINOIS

name: Ruben Gallego
subject: When I joined the Marines I had to answer a simple question
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
body:
The greatest thing I hope to pass on to my three kids and what I believe every American hopes to pass on to theirs is a single inheritance: democracy.
#

When I joined the Marine Corps more than 25 years ago, I had to answer a simple but terrifying question: was I willing to die for my country?
As the son of immigrants, someone who owed everything to this nation, I didn’t hesitate. The answer was yes.
Five years ago, I was forced to answer that question again.
When a violent mob stormed the Capitol, trying to overthrow our democracy, I had to decide: was I willing to put everything on the line, again, to defend this country and the Constitution I swore an oath to protect? And once again, the answer was yes.
I wasn’t alone. That night, in that room, Democrats and some Republicans stood together and said we were not going to let American democracy die. After witnessing the horrific violence, knowing the danger was still real, we returned to the floor and did our jobs. And because of that, our democracy survived.
The greatest thing I hope to pass on to my three kids and what I believe every American hopes to pass on to theirs is a single inheritance: democracy.
But democracy is not guaranteed. It only survives if people are willing to fight for it, protect it, and defend it. Just like we did that night.
To my colleagues who deny what happened, who minimize it, or worse who glorify it, think hard about the country you want our children to inherit.
And to you, Peter, I hope you haven’t given up. That’s what Donald Trump and the rest of them want. If we give up, they win.
I’ll fight for this country alone if I have to, but the only way we win is if we’re all in this together.
Can I count on you?
[**Make a donation of $5 so we have everything we need to take the fight to Trump over the next 11 months.**](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-em-jan26.Doc7Xe&refcode=em260106-1-160&t=1&akid=4058%2E734936%2E-k07b_)
[CHIP IN $5](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-em-jan26.Doc7Xe&refcode=em260106-1-160&t=2&akid=4058%2E734936%2E-k07b_)
Semper Fi,
Ruben

Paid For By Gallego for Arizona

Today marks **five years** since the events of January 6, 2021 – _a day that shook this nation’s foundations to its very core._

**Five years** since the violent, treasonous, Trump-incited insurrection on the Capitol.

**Five years** since Trump’s followers nearly overthrew the democratic, historic proceedings certifying the 2020 election results.

name: Mark Kelly
subject: They can threaten my rank and my pension — but they're not going to shut me up.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC | PO Box 96047, Washington, DC 20090 | **[dscc
body:
You swear an oath to the Constitution when you join the military — including the First Amendment right to free speech. We swore to defend the foundational principles of this country. I took that oath seriously 40 years ago, and I still do.
| | | Team:

Pete Hegseth decided he's going to censure me, and is threatening to demote me and reduce my pension.

I got shot at in service to this country. I missed holidays and birthdays. I commanded a space shuttle mission and launched to the International Space Station while my wife recovered from a gunshot wound to the head.

My rank and retirement are things that I earned through my service and sacrifice for this country. Generations of servicemembers have done the same. Pete Hegseth wants to send a message to every single one that he will come after them if they say something he doesn't like.

You swear an oath to the Constitution when you join the military — including the First Amendment right to free speech. We swore to defend the foundational principles of this country. I took that oath seriously 40 years ago, and I still do.

Pete Hegseth can threaten to reduce my rank and my pension. But I am not going to let him intimidate me, or anyone else. He is not going to stop me from defending our democracy from a president who is hellbent on destroying it.

If they think this is going to shut me up as we fight to take back the Senate, they've got another thing coming.

name: Pramila Jayapal
subject: Nobody should have to send texts like these to our loved ones
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
I was trapped in the House Gallery during the worst attack on the US Capitol since 1812.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20260106-5784-footer&t=1&akid=5784%2E420207%2EkVgtvf)
Peter,
Nobody should have to send texts like these to our loved ones:
[](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/51821?c0=pj-email-20260106-1.BJXrs1&refcode=em20260106-5784&t=2&akid=5784%2E420207%2EkVgtvf)
---
**But five years ago today, I did.**
I was trapped in the House Gallery during the worst attack on the US Capitol since 1812 — scared for my life, recovering from knee surgery, and armed with only my cane against insurrectionists beating on the doors, a sound I can still hear. I wasn’t sure I was going to make it out of the Capitol alive.
The trauma of that horrific day is still with me, and still with our country.
It’s a constant reminder of how fragile our democracy is, and why I’ll never stop fighting for accountability for those involved, including the assault’s culprit — Donald Trump — who continues to spew the same hateful, dangerous rhetoric from the Oval Office.
That’s why I spoke up in today’s hearing and will continue to tell my story so people don’t forget what happened on that day. And it’s why, along with other Members of Congress, I’m pushing forward with our case _Lee v. Trump_ to hold Trump accountable in the courts.
**[If you’re with me in the fight to protect our democracy from rogue leaders like Trump, will you chip in $3 or any amount you can today?](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20260106-1.KQQGQv&refcode=em20260106-5784&amount=3&t=3&akid=5784%2E420207%2EkVgtvf)**
**[Contribute $3](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20260106-1.KQQGQv&amount=3&refcode=em20260106-5784&t=22&akid=5784%2E420207%2EkVgtvf)**
Let’s send a clear message to the right-wing extremists in the White House: **Our democracy is worth fighting for.**
In solidarity,
Pramila Jayapal
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20260106-5784-footer&t=23&refcode2=5784_420207_kVgtvf&akid=5784%2E420207%2EkVgtvf)**
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.

name: Maxine Dexter
subject: January 6th was the starting bell, not the final fight.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maxine for Congress
body:
https://maxinefororegon.com/ [https://maxinefororegon.com/]
Five years ago today, an incensed mob stormed the Capitol in an attempt to overturn the results of the 2020 election. Since then, not only have Donald Trump and the Republican party continued to use inflammatory rhetoric, sow chaos, and enable corruption, but they used the power of the presidency to target political enemies.
This week, the United States Secretary of War Pete Hegseth announced plans to censure and demote Senator Mark Kelly of Arizona, a celebrated Navy veteran, over what the Trump administration is calling a “seditious video”. Senator Kelly is another in a long line of people targeted by the administration—from New York Attorney General Letitia James to former FBI Director James Comey to Gold Star families and fallen soldiers.
This is all part of the authoritarian playbook. Donald Trump is escalating, testing norms, and punishing dissent in real time, and it’s only going to get worse. January 6th was the starting bell, not the final fight.
On January 6th, we’re reminded that our democracy requires constant vigilance against authoritarian power grabs. We can’t let up, not for a second.
If you can, please chip in today to help put a check on Donald Trump’s power by flipping control of Congress this November. We need to send a message right now that we will not let Trump use his power to intimidate, punish, or silence anyone who stands up to him. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_med_email-26q1?refcode=x_260106_censure_act]

clean_body: [] Five years ago today, an incensed mob stormed the Capitol in an attempt to overturn the results of the 2020 election. Since then, not only have Donald Trump and the Republican party continued to use inflammatory rhetoric, sow chaos, and enable corruption, but they used the power of the presidency to target political enemies. This week, the United States Secretary of War Pete Hegseth announced plans to censure and demote Senator Mark Kelly of Arizona, a celebrated Navy veteran, over what the Trump administration is calling a “seditious video”. Senator Kelly is another in a long line of people targeted by the administration—from New York Attorney General Letitia James to former FBI Director James Comey to Gold Star families and fallen soldiers. This is all part of the authoritarian playbook. Donald Trump is escalating, testing norms, and punishing dissent in real time, and it’s only going to get worse. January 6th was the starting bell, not the final fight. On January 6th, we’re reminded that our democracy requires constant vigilance against authoritarian power grabs. We can’t let up, not for a second. If you can, please chip in today to help put a check on Donald Trump’s power by flipping control of Congress this November. We need to send a message right now that we will not let Trump use his power to intimidate, punish, or silence anyone who stands up to him. [**Before I explain more, will you become a Founding Donor of my re-election campaign by making a contribution of any amount now?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122804054/593509632/-2121819019?amount=10&refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20260106_PM2_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3Njk4NjFhZC00ZmViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tF7jlRQ2A2vlnaVbrVLh8Jf59vrT2RstRB-BaKmeIpU=) [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122804055/593509633/-2121819019?amount=10&refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20260106_PM2_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3Njk4NjFhZC00ZmViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tF7jlRQ2A2vlnaVbrVLh8Jf59vrT2RstRB-BaKmeIpU=) | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122804056/593509634/-2121819019?amount=10&refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20260106_PM2_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3Njk4NjFhZC00ZmViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tF7jlRQ2A2vlnaVbrVLh8Jf59vrT2RstRB-BaKmeIpU=)

I first ran for Congress in 2022 to preserve and protect our democracy from an assault by Donald Trump and the Republican party he leads. Little did I know then how existential that fight would become.
In 2019, I served as the Democrats’ Lead Counsel during Donald Trump’s _first_ impeachment investigation.
I saw firsthand how Donald Trump perceives our democratic ideals, values and institutions to be an obstacle to his absolute power. **And despite Trump’s best efforts to obstruct our investigation, I used my 10 years of experience to build a case that showed the American people how the president had abused the power of his office to undermine our democratic process in a bid to stay in control.**
Now, I’m officially launching my re-election campaign to continue the fight in Congress, because I want to once again be on the front lines holding Donald Trump accountable when Democrats take control of Congress in January 2027.
As Trump goes after every form of accountability for his lawless actions — the media, law enforcement, law firms, universities, non-profit organizations, and the courts — it’s not enough to have fighters in Congress. _We need fighters who know how to win — just like I did in the first impeachment._
[**But I can’t win this battle alone — and that’s why I’m hoping I can count on you to join me. Will you make a donation today as a Founding Donor of my re-election campaign?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122804057/593509635/-2121819019?amount=10&refcode=AU_DSG_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20260106_PM2_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDA4NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3Njk4NjFhZC00ZmViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tF7jlRQ2A2vlnaVbrVLh8Jf59vrT2RstRB-BaKmeIpU=)

name: Marsha Blackburn
subject: Peter, not to be too blunt, but I'm counting on you.
body:
That's why I need you more than ever.

| | | | [](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.)Peter, not to be too blunt, but I'm counting on you.
As a top supporter, you know I'm a fighter. I stood by President Trump when those we thought were patriots turned their backs on him. Yet, I refused to back down, and the Left will never forgive me for it.
But you've been with me every step of the way, patriot. Your support has been my shield against the RINOS and the radical Left. Now, they're coming at me with everything they've got with double the donors and double the dark money.
To be honest, tonight's critical fundraising goal is in jeopardy. I'm not one to get worried, but the numbers aren't looking good. That's why I need you more than ever, Peter. You're my lifeline.
If we don't meet our goal, we risk being forced to abandon our plans for 2026. I refuse to let the woke liberals win. We can't afford to lose a single battle in this war.
[Can I count on you to rush a donation before midnight? You're my salvation, Peter. I need you now.](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.) | [ DONATE ASAP ](https://trk.emails.marshablackburn.)

name: Powered by People
subject: What Axios just said about Beto
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
# [](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43706?t=1&akid=240584%2E444211%2EeGQxzS)
Did you see this?
With the 2026 midterms kicking into high gear, _Axios_ just published a story that says Beto "is focused on spearheading Powered by People to register voters and get Democrats elected in Texas."
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260106.CZltTx&refcode=ema20260106-240584&t=3&akid=240584%2E444211%2EeGQxzS)
They report that "he's been building Democratic infrastructure in Republican strongholds, holding rallies in Amarillo, Wichita Falls and Tyler as **he helped make sure Democrats were fielding a candidate in EVERY state and federal race on the 2026 ballot in Texas."**
And Beto’s not slowing down now. _Axios_ adds that with big races coming up throughout the state, "he will be rolling out voter registration events on college campuses—and is marshaling a ground game ahead of a North Texas special Senate race runoff at the end of January."
**This isn’t hype. It’s the real work that wins elections.**
Powered by People exists to meet Texans where they are, register new voters, and build grassroots power—county by county, campus by campus, block by block. We’re organizing on the ground, signing up volunteers, _and proving that when people are brought into our democracy, they make their voices heard._
This is how Texas changes—with sustained, people-powered organizing that expands the electorate and gives Texans a real say in their future.
Right now, we’re scaling up voter registration across the state, training volunteers, hiring organizers, and laying the groundwork to win critical races—starting with this month’s special election runoff in North Texas. Every clipboard, every volunteer shift, every college campus, every mile on the road takes resources.
[**If you believe in Beto’s work and that Texas is worth fighting for, chip in $5, $10, or whatever you can today to help fuel this organizing and turn momentum into victories up and down the ballot.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260106.CZltTx&refcode=ema20260106-240584&t=4&akid=240584%2E444211%2EeGQxzS)
**[Donate $3](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20260106.CZltTx&refcode=ema20260106-240584&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=23&akid=240584%2E444211%2EeGQxzS)**
Thank you,
