clean_body: Did you see this? With the 2026 midterms kicking into high gear, Axios just published a story that says Beto "is focused on spearheading Powered by People to register voters and get Democrats elected in Texas." They report that "he's been building Democratic infrastructure in Republican strongholds, holding rallies in Amarillo, Wichita Falls and Tyler as he helped make sure Democrats were fielding a candidate in EVERY state and federal race on the 2026 ballot in Texas." And Beto’s not slowing down now. Axios adds that with big races coming up throughout the state, "he will be rolling out voter registration events on college campuses—and is marshaling a ground game ahead of a North Texas special Senate race runoff at the end of January." This isn’t hype. It’s the real work that wins elections. Powered by People exists to meet Texans where they are, register new voters, and build grassroots power—county by county, campus by campus, block by block. We’re organizing on the ground, signing up volunteers, and proving that when people are brought into our democracy, they make their voices heard. This is how Texas changes—with sustained, people-powered organizing that expands the electorate and gives Texans a real say in their future. Right now, we’re scaling up voter registration across the state, training volunteers, hiring organizers, and laying the groundwork to win critical races—starting with this month’s special election runoff in North Texas. Every clipboard, every volunteer shift, every college campus, every mile on the road takes resources. Thank you, Powered by People

name: Mark Kelly
subject: Hegseth’s threats
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stand with Sanchez
body:
You swear an oath to the Constitution when you join the military — including the First Amendment right to free speech. We swore to defend the foundational principles of this country. I took that oath seriously 40 years ago, and I still do.

| Peter,

Pete Hegseth decided he's going to censure me, and is threatening to demote me and reduce my pension.

I got shot at in service to this country. I missed holidays and birthdays. I commanded a space shuttle mission and launched to the International Space Station while my wife recovered from a gunshot wound to the head.

My rank and retirement are things that I earned through my service and sacrifice for this country. Generations of servicemembers have done the same. Pete Hegseth wants to send a message to every single one that he will come after them if they say something he doesn’t like.

You swear an oath to the Constitution when you join the military — including the First Amendment right to free speech. We swore to defend the foundational principles of this country. I took that oath seriously 40 years ago, and I still do.

Pete Hegseth can threaten to reduce my rank and my pension. But I am not going to let him intimidate me, or anyone else. He is not going to stop me from defending our democracy from a president who is hellbent on destroying it.

If they think this is going to shut me up, they’ve got another thing coming.

[_**And if you’re still with me, please: Split a donation between Linda Sanchez and me so we can fight back.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122804527/593511059/17057241?refcode=BLU_EM_SAN_2026-01-06_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85ODI2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0YzI3NTVjYi01MWViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=yrvgvhw0re_7rtx928bbjO-UEeQKeFvNorhhDG-DXf4=)

name: Jessica Mackler, EMILYs List
subject: Representation delayed is representation denied
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Houston Women Win, a joint fundraising committee authorized by EMILYs List and Amanda Edwards for Congress**
body:
Houston families deserve the dignity of true representation |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122804574/593511305/-1181810701?refcode=WUEFHM03_20260106_HWW2_ALL_v1s2&sourceid=1057534&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMTM0ZDg0OC01MmViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mVJdIEPdvGU526MUDE9-4I7wafXRKdwVL9PHbn49R3o=) Peter, Houston's families have carried more than their share of uncertainty over the past year.

Without representation in Congress, families experiencing immigration emergencies, disaster recovery, healthcare appeals, or small business challenges had nowhere to turn for help. That was not an accident. It was the direct result of Gov. Greg Abbott's decision to delay giving this community a voice.

Amanda Edwards understands what this means in real life. When her father was diagnosed with cancer, her family faced the constant fear that insurance might not cover treatment and that they could lose everything. Far too many Texas families live with that same reality — one unexpected setback away from financial collapse.

Amanda is running to change this. She has dedicated her public service to expanding opportunity for small businesses, strengthening public transit, and increasing access to capital for women and minority entrepreneurs. She has approached governing with preparation, diligence, and a sincere commitment to responsibility.

We have also seen her leadership during moments of crisis. During Hurricane Harvey, Amanda went door to door checking on families, ensuring that seniors and neighbors who were trapped in their homes had food, medication, and access to emergency resources. That is what representation requires: responsiveness, compassion, and practical support.

The stakes of this runoff are clear. If Houston wants a representative who shows up, listens, and delivers, Amanda must have the resources to reach voters throughout this district. ****[**Please make a contribution today to help EMILYs List and Amanda Edwards and restore dignity, accountability, and service to TX-18:**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122804576/593511307/-1181810701?refcode=WUEFHM03_20260106_HWW2_ALL_v1s2&sourceid=1057534&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMTM0ZDg0OC01MmViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mVJdIEPdvGU526MUDE9-4I7wafXRKdwVL9PHbn49R3o=)**** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [DONATE $15](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122804580/593511310/-1181810701?express_lane=true&amount=15&refcode=WUEFHM03_20260106_HWW2_ALL_v1s2&sourceid=1057534&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMTM0ZDg0OC01MmViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mVJdIEPdvGU526MUDE9-4I7wafXRKdwVL9PHbn49R3o=) | [DONATE $35](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122804586/593511315/-1181810701?express_lane=true&amount=35&refcode=WUEFHM03_20260106_HWW2_ALL_v1s2&sourceid=1057534&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMTM0ZDg0OC01MmViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mVJdIEPdvGU526MUDE9-4I7wafXRKdwVL9PHbn49R3o=)

If Houston wants a representative who shows up, listens, and delivers, Amanda must have the resources to reach voters throughout this district. **= If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thank you for supporting this essential work, Jessica Mackler President, EMILYs List Paid for by Houston Women Win, a joint fundraising committee authorized by EMILYs List and Amanda Edwards for Congress** Houston Women Win P.O. Box 96612 Washington, DC 20077 United States Contributions or gifts to Houston Women Win are not tax deductible. From each contribution, 85% will be allocated to EMILYs List and 15% will be allocated to Amanda Edwards for Congress, designated for the run-off election. Any contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

name: Team Demand Justice PAC
subject: What they won’t say about Jan 6
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Demand Justice PAC
body:
[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20260106-61052&t=1001&akid=61052%2E1407376%2EZazLE0 ]Demand Justice PAC[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/54940?refcode=em20260106-61052&t=1002&akid=61052%2E1407376%2EZazLE0 ]Demand Justice PAC

Five years
ago today, our democracy was violently attacked.

On January 6, 2021, a mob incited by Donald Trump stormed the U.S. Capitol
to overturn an election he lost. The images are seared into history — and
they should still outrage us.

But this anniversary isn’t just about remembrance alone. It’s about what’s
happening right now.

In Trump’s second term, his judicial nominees are refusing to tell the
truth about January 6. Not equivocating. Not forgetting. Refusing.

When asked directly about the attack on the Capitol and the lie that
fueled it, one now-confirmed judge said this: “I was not present at the
U.S. Capitol at the time. I do not have personal knowledge of the
details.”

Shocking, right?

Trump has made the deal clear: if you want a lifetime seat on the federal
bench, you must prove your loyalty — by minimizing, excusing, or dodging
the truth about January 6.

And Senate Democrats are letting him get away with it. 18 Democratic
senators have voted to confirm at least one Trump judicial nominee who
refused to be honest about an attack on American democracy.

It's outrageous and it’s unacceptable.

January 6 was a test, and confirming judges who won’t even acknowledge the
truth about it is how we fail it.

This is exactly why Demand Justice exists. We are fighting Trump’s court
takeover. We are holding Democrats accountable when they cave. And we are
organizing to ensure our courts defend democracy — instead of laundering
its destruction.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20260106.7A8m88&refcode=em20260106-61052&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1003&akid=61052%2E1407376%2EZazLE0 ]If you believe January 6 mattered, if you believe the courts should
never be stacked with loyalists who excuse an attack on our democracy, and
if you’re ready to take a stand, chip in $5 today to Demand
Justice.

[ https://act.demandjustice.org/go/55459?c0=dj-em-20260106.7A8m88&amount=5&refcode=em20260106-61052&t=1022&akid=61052%2E1407376%2EZazLE0 ]Donate $5 ››

On January 6 of all days, there is no excuse for silence. No excuse for
cowardice or cover-ups. And no excuse for confirming Trump's crony judges.

In solidarity,

Team Demand Justice PAC

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

Receive Fewer Emails: https://act.demandjustice.org/survey/less-emails?t=1023&akid=61052%2E1407376%2EZazLE0
Update Your Information: https://act.demandjustice.org/signup/update-your-information/?t=1024&akid=61052%2E1407376%2EZazLE0
Unsubscribe: https://act.demandjustice.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?email=%%email%%&t=1025&akid=61052%2E1407376%2EZazLE0
Email us: feedback@demandjustice.org

Paid for by Demand Justice PAC
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE, #192, Washington DC 20003
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
Contributions or gifts to Demand Justice PAC are not tax deductible.

subject: President Trump Frees Venezuela from a Tyrant
body:
Next Iran and CUBA LIBRE!

---|---|---

---
# [President Trump Frees Venezuela from a Tyrant](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=365201&post_id=183729070&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgzNzI5MDcwLCJpYXQiOjE3Njc3NDAxNTcsImV4cCI6MTc3MDMzMjE1NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTM2NTIwMSIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.HcziSqHrZF-ubJalpko1hKNAWPHgreJUdmdzNYzGc2w)
### Next Iran and CUBA LIBRE!
| | [Joseph Bentivegna](https://substack.com/@jfbentivegna)
---
| Jan 6
---
| [](https://substack.com/@jfbentivegna)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=365201&post_id=183729070&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgzNzI5MDcwLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2Nzc0MDE1NywiZXhwIjoxNzcwMzMyMTU3LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzY1MjAxIiwic3ViIjoicmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.QNlFdOYs7XS2s7iTfBR9Fbs5ogV6yPMgE59OD5curgs&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=365201&post_id=183729070&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgzNzI5MDcwLCJpYXQiOjE3Njc3NDAxNTcsImV4cCI6MTc3MDMzMjE1NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTM2NTIwMSIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.HcziSqHrZF-ubJalpko1hKNAWPHgreJUdmdzNYzGc2w&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=365201&post_id=183729070&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgzNzI5MDcwLCJpYXQiOjE3Njc3NDAxNTcsImV4cCI6MTc3MDMzMjE1NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTM2NTIwMSIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.HcziSqHrZF-ubJalpko1hKNAWPHgreJUdmdzNYzGc2w)
---

---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/jfbentivegna/p/president-trump-frees-venezuela-from?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

January 6, 2026
In an aggressive move that has reshuffled international politics, President Trump ordered a military assault on Venezuela and arrested the socialist thug President Maduro and his wife after an [American indictment](https://substack.com/redirect/4fb8628d-1027-4eab-aedd-959c83598039?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) accused them of drug trafficking. No Americans were killed but some Venezuelans and at least thirty Cubans were killed
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c9bdd1f6-84b5-438a-bcf7-12085054b4d3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
. Maduro after being captured

But this was the easy part. As General Colin Powell famously observed after President George W. Bush invaded Iraq, "If you break it, you own it."
President Trump now owns Venezuela. If a stable democratic government can be established quickly, President Trump will smell like a rose and tyrants in Iran, Cuba and Nicaragua could be overthrown. If not, not only will he be blamed for feckless "gringo imperialism," but tyrants across the planet will be emboldened.

But how did we get here?
Venezuela is in northern South America and has a gorgeous Caribbean coastline. It is blessed with abundant natural resources and so much oil that it is a founding member of OPEC (Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries). Military dictatorships were the norm, but democracy was making progress so that by the 1950s, Venezuela was the fourth wealthiest country on the planet.

But in the 1990s, oil prices collapsed leading to austerity measures that led to discontent and the founder of the Revolutionary Bolivarian Movement-200, Hugo Chavez, to attempt a coup. Although unsuccessful, Chavez was able to win the Presidency in 1998 and instituted socialist policies. Awash in oil money, Chavez spent lavishly while Venezuela thrived. But as Margaret Thatcher said, "They [Socialists] always run out of other people's money".
And that is exactly what happened.
Chavez passed away from cancer in 2013 and was replaced by his henchman Nicolas Maduro. But by now, [socialist policies](https://substack.com/redirect/8875f7c4-0d70-4bee-844a-3656b405586a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) sent Venezuela's economy into a freefall and Maduro did what tyrants always do when their power is threatened - [confiscate the guns of the population. ](https://substack.com/redirect/3f248dd4-7d0e-4b6a-8cab-8f608973e32b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

Now with unchallenged power, Maduro was able to [brutalize the helpless unarmed](https://substack.com/redirect/327fa6e9-4095-45c2-8645-cf15d7bee040?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Venezuelan people. The economy continued to collapse, inflation skyrocketed to 10,000%, starvation became rampant and 14-year-old girls were forced into prostitution to support their families. Twenty percent of the population, seven million people, fled the country, including hundreds of thousands illegally to the United States.
In the 2024 election, a courageous woman, Maria Machado, arose to challenge Maduro for the presidency. But she was so popular that corrupt judges barred her from running for office. She was replaced by Edmondo Gonzalez of the Unitary Platform political alliance.

But then Gonzalez won by over 70% Thus the [corrupt judges](https://substack.com/redirect/d2b25c06-2981-4920-ad9c-abebdbf2e0f2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) declared the election invalid and issued an arrest warrant for Gonalez who was granted asylum in Spain.

Meanwhile, the economy collapsed further and Venezuelans continued to flee while Maduro and his henchmen developed a lucrative drug smuggling operation to United States.

President Trump wanted him removed and started blowing up the drug runners' boats while stopping Venezuela's oil shipments. He gave Maduro a chance to leave but Maduro refused and has now suffered the consequences.

But now what? President Trump said we are going to run the country. But we have no troops deployed there. How are we going to run the country?
President Trump stated he will work with the Vice President Delcy Rodriguez who is now the President. But as soon as she got off the phone with President Trump, this avowed socialist stated President Trump's action was an "illegitimate military aggression" and characterized the removal of Maduro as "kidnapping." The military has pledged their support to her and meanwhile, shortages of food are escalating.

Is this running the country?
Maria Machado states she wants to return to Venezuela and run in a free and fair election. But what if President Rodriquez and the military don't want elections?

Perhaps continued stopping of oil shipments will force Rodriguez and the military to capitulate. And perhaps not.

But President Trump has painted himself in a corner if he does not have a plan to stabilize Venezuela without the use of American troops. And if he places American troops in Venezuela, how long will it be until one or more are killed? How will the average American react when that happens? How long will Venezuela and the rest of Latin America tolerate "gringo imperialism?"
When Fidel Castro died in 2016, the political establishment offered little criticism of his legacy, but not President Trump who stated: "Today marks the passage of a brutal dictator who oppressed his own people for nearly six decades. Fidel Castro's legacy is one of firing squads, theft, unimaginable suffering, poverty and the denial of human rights.
President Kennedy tried to overthrow Castro with the disastrous Bay of Pigs invasion. Will removing Maduro be President Trump's Bay of Pigs? Or will the President be able to stabilize Venezuela and then remove this evil Cuban communist government that has totally rapped and decimated this once thriving county? And will this momentum remove the unpopular Ayatollah in Iran?

President Trump's legacy and geopolitics will depend on how these events unfold.
Read Dr. Bentivegna's books for free:
Two novels on Fairfield County politics and race relations, [The Lords of Greenwich ](https://substack.com/redirect/be82e3c7-c87f-41b6-83fd-7bb3140ad9d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)and [Respect.](https://substack.com/redirect/b3730a4b-3725-452e-8793-10a25b805769?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Dr. Bentivegna's year in Haiti as a young doctor. [The Neglected and Abused: A Physician's Year in Haiti.](https://substack.com/redirect/d21e6f52-6c0e-4d73-ba17-0313a534906c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Contact Dr. Bentivegna: [jfbentivegna@gmail.com](https://substack.com/redirect/ecad32bb-04bf-43f6-bcd3-68c687057cd2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

name: Gina Hinojosa
subject: New Year’s Resolutions
body:
I only have one resolution for 2026.
# [](https://ginafortexas.com/?refcode=em_ms_gh_header)[](https://ginafortexas.com/?refcode=em_ms_gh_header)
Peter, a lot of us make resolutions at the outset of a new year — getting in shape, reading more books, spending more time outdoors or with family.
This year, I only have one resolution. And it’s a pretty big one:
**My 2026 mission is to defeat Greg Abbott and deliver new leadership to Texas.**
But unlike some of our goals, this is one I can’t accomplish on my own. Flipping a state as big as Texas is going to require all hands on deck — and competing with Abbott’s $90 million warchest means our grassroots support needs to be strong.
If you’re with me, Peter, can I count on you to step up and back this campaign?
**[Contribute $5 or more right now to help us prepare to fight — _and win._](https://act.ginafortexas.com/go/625?c0=em_ms_gh_20260106_1_resolutions.AOOA3M&refcode=em20260106-375&amount=5&t=1&akid=375%2E299639%2Ea3Ai8N)**
[Donate $5 »](https://act.ginafortexas.com/go/625?c0=em_ms_gh_20260106_1_resolutions.AOOA3M&amount=5&refcode=em20260106-375&t=20&akid=375%2E299639%2Ea3Ai8N)
With gratitude,
Gina

[ Donate » ](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_gh_footer?refcode=em20260106-1&t=21&refcode2=375_299639_a3Ai8N&akid=375%2E299639%2Ea3Ai8N)

subject: What’s next after this major victory
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
Together, we can and will protect democracy from Republican attempts to gerrymander across the country.
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122804933/593512729/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImFmNmI4ZTc1LTUzZWItZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=_Ktms_UJkx0RO5Ajv4Mt0yeYmbMn2oeVcwVc4qjDeqA=&emci=73750aae-1deb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=af6b8e75-53eb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=8431045)
---

Peter, **One of the most egregiously gerrymandered proposed maps in the country is no more.** Last month, the Indiana State Senate rejected a mid-decade gerrymander that would’ve given Republicans more congressional seats and silenced voters. Through a bipartisan vote, which included a majority of Republicans, leaders in Indiana rejected this extreme map from President Trump and his anti-democratic coalition. Legislators stood up despite threats of violence against them and their families, and Trump administration vows to strip federal funding from Indiana. As AG Holder said, “the courage demonstrated by legislators in Indiana is a hopeful sign and perhaps an indication that the commitment to democracy will outweigh partisan loyalty heading into 2026.” This is a major rebuttal to the nationwide redistricting crisis. And we will need more responses just like it in this new year, especially as states like Florida, New Hampshire, and others could begin the process of redrawing their congressional maps this month. [**Give today to strengthen the organizational and political power of NDRC to stop anti-democratic efforts to reshape our maps and rig the midterms in 2026.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122804934/593512730/1128139581?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_IndianaFightRedux_email_20260106-NDRC-Tier1-IndianaFightRedux_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20260106-NDRC-Tier1-IndianaFightRedux_&utm_campaign=IndianaFightRedux&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImFmNmI4ZTc1LTUzZWItZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=_Ktms_UJkx0RO5Ajv4Mt0yeYmbMn2oeVcwVc4qjDeqA=) This win shows that, while the Trump administration and Republican leaders are putting intense pressure on states to gerrymander, mobilized efforts from voters are working. Still, we know that this fight is far from over. In the year ahead, President Trump will encourage more efforts to gerrymander. Our collective action must continue to sound the alarm and prevent lawmakers from stripping fair representation from voters across the country. **Our next step is to ensure that what happened in Indiana isn’t just a one-off victory, but a signaling point for more action to end this power grab. Are you in?** [**Make a gift to help empower voters to fight back against Trump’s gerrymandering scheme.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122804935/593512731/1128139581?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_IndianaFightRedux_email_20260106-NDRC-Tier1-IndianaFightRedux_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20260106-NDRC-Tier1-IndianaFightRedux_&utm_campaign=IndianaFightRedux&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImFmNmI4ZTc1LTUzZWItZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=_Ktms_UJkx0RO5Ajv4Mt0yeYmbMn2oeVcwVc4qjDeqA=) Thank you for making wins like the one we saw in Indiana possible, Team NDRC | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122804936/593512732/1128139581?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_IndianaFightRedux_email_20260106-NDRC-Tier1-IndianaFightRedux_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20260106-NDRC-Tier1-IndianaFightRedux_&utm_campaign=IndianaFightRedux&amount=30&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImFmNmI4ZTc1LTUzZWItZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=_Ktms_UJkx0RO5Ajv4Mt0yeYmbMn2oeVcwVc4qjDeqA=)

clean_body: Together, we can and will protect democracy from Republican attempts to gerrymander across the country. | Peter, One of the most egregiously gerrymandered proposed maps in the country is no more. Last month, the Indiana State Senate rejected a mid-decade gerrymander that would’ve given Republicans more congressional seats and silenced voters. Through a bipartisan vote, which included a majority of Republicans, leaders in Indiana rejected this extreme map from President Trump and his anti-democratic coalition. Legislators stood up despite threats of violence against them and their families, and Trump administration vows to strip federal funding from Indiana. As AG Holder said, “the courage demonstrated by legislators in Indiana is a hopeful sign and perhaps an indication that the commitment to democracy will outweigh partisan loyalty heading into 2026.” This is a major rebuttal to the nationwide redistricting crisis. And we will need more responses just like it in this new year, especially as states like Florida, New Hampshire, and others could begin the process of redrawing their congressional maps this month. = This win shows that, while the Trump administration and Republican leaders are putting intense pressure on states to gerrymander, mobilized efforts from voters are working. Still, we know that this fight is far from over. In the year ahead, President Trump will encourage more efforts to gerrymander. Our collective action must continue to sound the alarm and prevent lawmakers from stripping fair representation from voters across the country. Our next step is to ensure that what happened in Indiana isn’t just a one-off victory, but a signaling point for more action to end this power grab. Are you in? = Thank you for making wins like the one we saw in Indiana possible, Team NDRC | = = | | Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President. | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|--- National Democratic Redistricting Committee 712 H St NE, #25 Washington, DC 20002 United States if you'd like to receive fewer emails from the NDRC. Text MAPS to 36787, STOP to end. Msg and Data rates may apply

Peter,
We’re five days away from our first major campaign milestone.
On January 11, New York’s public matching-funds clock resets and our goal is to qualify by then. It’s ambitious, especially for a campaign that just launched a few weeks ago. But it’s absolutely within reach, and hitting it would send a powerful message: this campaign is ready to compete statewide.
To qualify, we need 1,000 New Yorkers to contribute $100,000 in small-dollar donations . We’re close but we need your help to get over the line.
If you can, please chip in $5 today [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/polarplungeemail] .
For New Yorkers, that $5 is matched 6-to-1, becoming $30 and those small gifts are what unlock the public funds that allow us to reach voters across the state.
We’re running to invest in clean energy, fix the MTA, and launch an Opportunity Trust Fund so every New Yorker has a real shot at the future. Your contribution today helps make that vision possible.
Thank you for being part of this early momentum.
— Adem

name: Mark Ronchetti
subject: Venezuelan dictator captured — Democrats call for impeachment
body:
Plus: NYC's new mayor wants "collectivism" — that's what destroyed Venezuela | New for dpwillis67@gmail.com

| | | | | |
---
[|
---](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=f8368b49a1&e=567664f14c)
| | | | | | Venezuela's dictator is in a Brooklyn jail cell. Venezuelans around the world are dancing in the streets. Democrats are calling for impeachment. [And protesters in Albuquerque are saying "it's all about oil."](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=842675239b&e=567664f14c) Then a Venezuelan American journalist who lost her father during the crisis spoke up. **You need to hear what she said.**
---
[|
---](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=e6759d9903&e=567664f14c)
| |
🇻🇪 **MARIANA ATENCIO RESPONDS TO "IT'S FOR OIL"**
Her father died because there was no medicine. She watched her country collapse under 25 years of dictatorship. 8 million people fled — including her family.
This weekend, protesters looked into a camera and said, "We all know it's for oil."
She had something to say about that. Her voice cracks when she talks about it. She's not performing — she's lived it.
[**We play the full clip.**](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=450b02338a&e=567664f14c)**This is the moment everyone needs to see.**
---
| [Stream Episode Now](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=a51e28c6df&e=567664f14c)
---
| |
🎭 **CHRIS MURPHY SAID THE EXACT OPPOSITE ONE YEAR AGO**
Senator Chris Murphy went on TV this weekend and said the operation was "mostly about oil."
So we pulled tape from one year ago. Same senator. Same country.
He called the dictator "brutal." Said he "probably lost in a landslide." Warned that more refugees would flood our border if he stayed in power.
[**We play both clips back to back.**](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=7fbcec2910&e=567664f14c)**No commentary needed.

** ⚠️ **NYC'S NEW MAYOR WANTS "COLLECTIVISM" — THAT'S WHAT DESTROYED VENEZUELA**
Zohran Mamdani just became NYC's mayor. Two days into the job, he called the Venezuela operation "an act of war."
Then we found his inaugural address: "We will replace rugged individualism with the warmth of collectivism."
Then we found a 2021 video where he says — smiling — "the end goal is seizing the means of production."
That's exactly what Hugo Chavez said. We walk through the full timeline of how Venezuela collapsed — and why it matters that an American mayor is saying the same things.
---
| [Stream Episode Now](https://nodoubtaboutitpodcast.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d2649c77b4f4f1f9d512804d2&id=5475865be2&e=567664f14c)
---
| |
📊 **SAME REGION, OPPOSITE RESULTS — VENEZUELA VS. GUYANA**
What happens when a country does the opposite of socialism?
Guyana is right next door to Venezuela. Same region. Same resources.
In 2010, Guyana's GDP per capita was $3,800. Venezuela's was $11,000.
Today? Guyana is at $31,000. Venezuela is at $3,000.
We break down exactly what happened — and why this comparison matters for what comes next.
---
|
---

name: Angela Alsobrooks
subject: My mission is crystal-clear
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alsobrooks for Senate
body:
|
---
[ ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/2092?t=1&akid=3425%2E52803%2E2Ts4gc)
| | Five years ago, a violent mob stormed the Capitol to overturn the results of a lawful election at the behest of the President of the United States. We owe a debt of gratitude to the brave men and women of the Capitol Police who stood in the breach in defense of our democracy. That dark day was the start of a dark chapter in our history. This isn't the first dark chapter we’ve ever experienced. It's not the worst we’ve experienced. It won’t be the last we’ll ever experience. **It is, however, our responsibility to turn the page.** The toxic greed, hate, and extremism exposed that day remains in our political bloodstream. Our failed president wants to keep poisoning us, keep dividing us. He'll start an illegal war. He'll play games with the Epstein files. He'll gaslight us about the economy. He'll let people die of preventable diseases in droves. **But I won’t be distracted by a clown and his circus.****My mission is crystal-clear.** This year, I'll continue my fight for law and order, health care, education, jobs, affordable housing, and community safety. And over the next five years, I’ll do whatever it takes to pass down a free nation to our kids and their kids after them. **We are going to outlast this president and be the authors of America’s next great chapter.** _Together._ With love and gratitude, Angela Alsobrooks | | | [Donate](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/39308?t=9&amount=25&akid=3425%2E52803%2E2Ts4gc)
---
| | [ ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/89?t=2&akid=3425%2E52803%2E2Ts4gc)
---
| | An important update from Team Alsobrooks:

Angela is so thankful for this grassroots team’s support and is eager to get to work for Marylanders in the Senate. That’s why you’ll be receiving fewer fundraising emails from us moving forward, in an effort to not clog up your inbox for the next six years.

Instead, we’d rather send you emails you want to read – like updates on Angela’s work in the Senate, or rundowns of key issues and news stories from Washington.

But to make this possible, we’re relying on supporters like you to consider making a small, monthly recurring contribution of $3 or even $5 to keep our operations running while Angela is busy fighting for Maryland families.

[Make a recurring contribution to Angela Alsobrooks' campaign here.](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/33703?t=3&akid=3425%2E52803%2E2Ts4gc)
---
We believe that emails are the best way for our organization to stay in touch with supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [click here.](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/signup/fewer-emails/?t=4&akid=3425%2E52803%2E2Ts4gc)
| | [ Chip in a one-time donation ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/4693?t=5&akid=3425%2E52803%2E2Ts4gc)
---

name: Scott Peters
subject: Still standing up to extremism
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scott Peters for Congress
body:
---
| | Peter,

Five years ago today, our nation experienced one of the darkest moments in its history. On January 6, 2021, a violent mob, incited by Donald Trump and his allies, stormed the U.S. Capitol in an attempt to overturn a free and fair election and prevent the peaceful transfer of power.

I was there that day, sitting in the House gallery as we debated the certification of the Electoral College votes to make Joe Biden President of the United States. What followed over the next several hours is something I will never forget.

We were abruptly told the Capitol had been breached. Gas masks beneath our seats were opened in anticipation of tear gas. Eventually, we were ordered to drop to the floor as Capitol Police barricaded the doors with furniture, guns drawn, waiting for whatever came next. We evacuated through tunnels to an undisclosed location and were held there for hours with little information.

Later, I learned that people in costumes mocked the Speaker’s Office and desecrated the People’s House. I learned that someone had been killed. And I learned that she was from San Diego, from the district I represent.

That day shook our nation to its core. We owe an immense debt to the Capitol Police and law enforcement officers who risked their lives to protect us and our democracy.

**Five years later, one thing is painfully clear: the forces that fueled January 6 have not disappeared. They did not quietly retreat. Instead, many are still working to undermine faith in our elections, restrict access to the ballot box, and cling to power at any cost.**

Words matter. Lies have consequences. When leaders refuse to tell the truth and instead inflame fear and grievance, democracy is put at risk.

Millions of Americans voted lawfully in 2020. Those votes were counted, audited, challenged in court, and upheld by judges appointed by Democrats and Republicans, all the way to the Supreme Court. The Electoral College voted. Joe Biden won. Even Republican election officials, state legislatures, and leaders acknowledged the results. Yet the lies continued, and the damage followed.

As we mark this fifth anniversary, we have a responsibility to remember what happened, to honor those who defended our democracy, and to recommit ourselves to protecting it. That means standing up to extremism, defending the right to vote, and demanding accountability from those who would undermine our Constitution.

I love this country. I believe deeply in this democracy that generations before us fought and sacrificed to protect. And I will continue working with my colleagues to ensure that January 6 is never repeated.

Thank you for staying engaged, for remembering, and for standing up for our most fundamental values.

Scott

name: Jon Ossoff via Off the Sidelines PAC
subject: I am MAGA’s #1 target without a doubt
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Off the Sidelines PAC
body:
secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_q12026_ots-ossoff-split?amount= [secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_q12026_ots-ossoff-split?amount=]

Let me lay this out, clear as day:

I am the only Democratic Senator up for reelection in a state Trump won in 2024.

This Senate race in Georgia will be the most competitive and most expensive in the nation.

If you’re outraged by what’s happening to our country — and you’re ready to fight — please split $5 between my reelection campaign and Off the Sidelines today. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_q12026_ots-ossoff-split?amount=]

SPLIT A DONATION >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_q12026_ots-ossoff-split?amount=]

Otherwise, let me remind you why I ran for Senate — and why this moment is just as urgent.

Before I was elected, I led investigations of corruption and human rights abuses worldwide. We exposed wrongdoing in countries where criticizing the government is dangerous, where whistleblowers and witnesses will only meet in secret, where protest carries the risk of prosecution.

More and more, the atmosphere under Trump reminded me of those places where dissent was silenced by intimidation.

Trump’s rise wasn’t just a crisis. It was a warning.

I ran for Senate because I saw the danger in electing someone so profoundly unfit for the presidency and I wanted to confront the corruption that made his rise possible.

I also ran — years before the Dobbs decision — because it was already clear the GOP was coming for reproductive rights. I saw Georgia as the front line in that fight. With a six-week ban now in place here, and Republicans pushing for a national abortion ban, it still is.

Now this President is staging a power grab unprecedented in our history:

* He’s freed violent criminals who attacked the Capitol on his behalf.
* Purged independent watchdogs across government.
* Stacked key agencies with loyalists — and called on them to investigate his political opponents.
* Defied and threatened federal judges.
* Undermined the press.

Trump’s goal isn’t just revenge. He wants to use the government to crush the opposition. And now, Americans are living in fear of their own government.

This is not a drill and it’s not a bad dream. As citizens, it is the test of our lifetimes.

In 2020, the pundits said flipping a Republican Senate seat in Georgia couldn’t be done. But we built one of the strongest voter mobilization programs in Senate campaign history — and we won. That victory won the Senate majority.

Now we must win again. And we will win again.

Just like last time, I can’t do it alone.

So I’m asking:

Please split a contribution between my reelection campaign and Off the Sidelines today, of $5 or any amount that works for you. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_q12026_ots-ossoff-split?amount=]

The average online donation to our campaign is around $30. Many give less. Some give more.

All that matters is that lots and lots of people are contributing what they can to power our grassroots campaign. Please, contribute today.

CONTRIBUTE >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_q12026_ots-ossoff-split?amount=]

Thanks for being part of this team.

As always,

Jon Ossoff© 2025 | Paid for by Off the Sidelines PAC

clean_body: secure.actblue.com/donate/locq12026ots-ossoff-split?amount= [secure.actblue.com/donate/locq12026ots-ossoff-split?amount=] Let me lay this out, clear as day: I am the only Democratic Senator up for reelection in a state Trump won in 2024. This Senate race in Georgia will be the most competitive and most expensive in the nation. If you’re outraged by what’s happening to our country — and you’re ready to fight — please split $5 between my reelection campaign and Off the Sidelines today. [=] SPLIT A DONATION >> [=] Otherwise, let me remind you why I ran for Senate — and why this moment is just as urgent. Before I was elected, I led investigations of corruption and human rights abuses worldwide. We exposed wrongdoing in countries where criticizing the government is dangerous, where whistleblowers and witnesses will only meet in secret, where protest carries the risk of prosecution. More and more, the atmosphere under Trump reminded me of those places where dissent was silenced by intimidation. Trump’s rise wasn’t just a crisis. It was a warning. I ran for Senate because I saw the danger in electing someone so profoundly unfit for the presidency and I wanted to confront the corruption that made his rise possible. I also ran — years before the Dobbs decision — because it was already clear the GOP was coming for reproductive rights. I saw Georgia as the front line in that fight. With a six-week ban now in place here, and Republicans pushing for a national abortion ban, it still is. Now this President is staging a power grab unprecedented in our history: He’s freed violent criminals who attacked the Capitol on his behalf. Purged independent watchdogs across government. Stacked key agencies with loyalists — and called on them to investigate his political opponents. Defied and threatened federal judges. * Undermined the press. Trump’s goal isn’t just revenge. He wants to use the government to crush the opposition. And now, Americans are living in fear of their own government. This is not a drill and it’s not a bad dream. As citizens, it is the test of our lifetimes. In 2020, the pundits said flipping a Republican Senate seat in Georgia couldn’t be done. But we built one of the strongest voter mobilization programs in Senate campaign history — and we won. That victory won the Senate majority. Now we must win again. And we will win again. Just like last time, I can’t do it alone. So I’m asking: Please split a contribution between my reelection campaign and Off the Sidelines today, of $5 or any amount that works for you. [=] The average online donation to our campaign is around $30. Many give less. Some give more. All that matters is that lots and lots of people are contributing what they can to power our grassroots campaign. Please, contribute today. CONTRIBUTE >> [=] Thanks for being part of this team. As always, Jon Ossoff© 2025 | Paid for by Off the Sidelines PAC

name: David Hogg
subject: Five years ago
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
We must defeat Trumpism for good.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20260106-1&t=1&akid=3018%2E587540%2EXyiMXK)
Peter,
Five years ago today, white nationalists stormed the Capitol with weapons in an attempt to overturn the results of the 2020 election. Nine deaths were linked to the attack.
Despite the coup attempt, we all know Donald Trump returned to power in the 2024 election. And over the past year, we’ve seen just how dangerous, chaotic, and downright deranged his second term is going to be. From terrorizing immigrant communities, using the military to do the bidding of oil companies, defunding programs created to prevent gun violence, to his total cover up of the Epstein files — there’s no shortage of reminders.
**Let today be a reminder of what we’re up against, Peter.**
This fight is about more than one man — it’s about the entire political movement that would rather see our country burn to the ground than admit they were wrong.
We have to defeat Trumpism for good. And in order to repair our democracy and build a better future, we must weed out the far-right extremism in Congress and beyond by directly challenging the hopelessness that Republicans feed off of to stay in power. That starts with this year’s midterms, by electing a new generation of young leaders who will advance a progressive vision for the future. Leaders who won’t take money from the very special interests and corporations that continue to fund many of the members of Congress who were directly involved in denying the results of the 2020 election.
Thank you for supporting the next generation of leaders.
David Hogg

# [Under Trump, U.S. Manufacturing Is in Retreat](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3722532&post_id=183597387&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgzNTk3Mzg3LCJpYXQiOjE3Njc3NDQwNjgsImV4cCI6MTc3MDMzNjA2OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTM3MjI1MzIiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Q1cFCZycis2_HcTq2oVwVwMdywS7x4lTGOaiKpp8MWw)
### Ten straight months of contraction--and no slogans can hide the math

| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/blueamp/p/trump-and-the-art-of-industrial-sabotage?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ef94ccfb-fc3c-489e-88d9-79a07da49323?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ef94ccfb-fc3c-489e-88d9-79a07da49323?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_**By David Shuster**_
The tragedy of American manufacturing under President Donald Trump is not merely that it is shrinking. It is that U.S. manufacturing is shrinking under a lunatic who never tires of boasting that he alone can make U.S. manufacturing great again. The boast, like so many in the Trump era, has aged horribly.
Under President Joe Biden, manufacturing did something unusual in modern politics. It worked.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ef94ccfb-fc3c-489e-88d9-79a07da49323?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Output climbed to record levels. Investment poured into factories, supply chains stabilized following the Covid hurdles, and the dull, unglamorous business of making things regained a measure of dignity. The boost in U.S. manufacturing was achieved not with chest-thumping speeches or tariff tantrums, but with policies designed for the real world--predictability, infrastructure, and a government that did not wake up each morning looking for a trade war to fight.
Then came Trump 2.0 and with it the old MAGA chaos: tariffs waved like magic wands, certainty treated as weakness, and economic policy reduced to a series of impulsive taunts thrown at U.S. allies and rivals alike.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ef94ccfb-fc3c-489e-88d9-79a07da49323?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The result has been a manufacturing sector retreat--contracting for ten straight months, a sustained decline that no amount of Trump shrieking and podium pounding can wish away.
As Barron's reports, The Institute for Supply Management's (ISM) manufacturing index fell to 47.9 from November's 48.2 reading, the lowest of 2025 despite modest improvements in employment
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ef94ccfb-fc3c-489e-88d9-79a07da49323?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The consistent decline since Trump 2.0 took office is particularly noteworthy when you consider Old man Orange sold us his tariffs in 2025 as medicine. Trump said the tariffs would discipline foreign competitors, resurrect factories, and restore American industrial dominance.
In practice, the Trump tariffs have functioned more like a recurring fever: raising costs for manufacturers, snarling supply chains, and making long-term planning an exercise in guesswork.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ef94ccfb-fc3c-489e-88d9-79a07da49323?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
When steel, aluminum, and components grow more expensive overnight--and might grow more expensive again tomorrow--investment does not flourish. It hides.
Manufacturing thrives on characteristics that sound boring: stability, clarity, and predictability. In other words, manufacturers need to have confidence that the rules will not change before the next quarterly report. Trump offers none of this. Instead, Trump delivers uncertainty as spectacle, a governing style that treats unpredictability as a strength. The MAGA crowd may roar. But economists wince and U.S. factories quietly scale back.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ef94ccfb-fc3c-489e-88d9-79a07da49323?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
There is a special irony in watching Trump align himself with American manufacturing workers while presiding over policies that undermine the very industries Trump claims to champion. Protectionism, according to Trump, is a kind of muscular patriotism. In reality, protectionism is a tax. It is paid by producers, passed on to consumers, and absorbed by workers when factory orders diminish and shifts are cut. It is nationalism for people who do not read balance sheets, including the MAGA nutjobs.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ef94ccfb-fc3c-489e-88d9-79a07da49323?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Meanwhile, the comparison to the previous administration lingers like an inconvenient footnote Trump desperately keeps trying to erase. Manufacturing did not boom under Biden because he insulted trading partners or slapped tariffs on impulse.
Manufacturing grew to record levels during the Biden administration because companies believed the future would resemble the present closely enough to justify investment. Confidence, not bravado, did the heavy lifting.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ef94ccfb-fc3c-489e-88d9-79a07da49323?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
These days, Trump claims that America's economy under Biden was "dead." And in a suspension of reality and common sense that only Trump could imagine, Trump says we are the hottest economic nation in the world.
When confronted with actual reality and statistics from the administration's own government agencies, Trump's defenders insist the manufacturing and industrial pain is temporary. Trump economic advisor Kevin Hassett and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent maintain that the ongoing contractions are a necessary sacrifice on the altar of future industrial greatness.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ef94ccfb-fc3c-489e-88d9-79a07da49323?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
This is the same argument made by every economic quack throughout the industrial age: endure the harm now and trust the miracle will arrive later. Manufacturers, being practical and rationale people, are not buying it.
So what we are left with in the U.S. under Trump is a familiar pattern: loud promises, thin results, and an insistence that basic facts are wrong, rather than the policy.
Manufacturing is not declining because it lacks patriotism. It is declining because it lacks a U.S. President who understands that modern industry is built on cooperation, predictability, and trust--not haphazard tariffs.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ef94ccfb-fc3c-489e-88d9-79a07da49323?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
If American manufacturing is going to recover, it will not be rescued by slogans or by a deranged President Trump who mistakes chaos for strength. U.S. manufacturing will recover once White House policy is grounded in economic sense rather than applause lines. Until then, Trump's vision of industrial revival remains a noisy farce, highlighted by bluster, and collapsing under basic arithmetic.
The only thing Trump is growing in U.S. manufacturing, like so much else, is decline.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ef94ccfb-fc3c-489e-88d9-79a07da49323?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|

clean_body: Ten straight months of contraction--and no slogans can hide the math | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Ten straight months of contraction--and no slogans can hide the math | | and | Jan 7 | | | ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- By David Shuster The tragedy of American manufacturing under President Donald Trump is not merely that it is shrinking. It is that U.S. manufacturing is shrinking under a lunatic who never tires of boasting that he alone can make U.S. manufacturing great again. The boast, like so many in the Trump era, has aged horribly. Under President Joe Biden, manufacturing did something unusual in modern politics. It worked. | | ---|---|--- Output climbed to record levels. Investment poured into factories, supply chains stabilized following the Covid hurdles, and the dull, unglamorous business of making things regained a measure of dignity. The boost in U.S. manufacturing was achieved not with chest-thumping speeches or tariff tantrums, but with policies designed for the real world--predictability, infrastructure, and a government that did not wake up each morning looking for a trade war to fight. Then came Trump 2.0 and with it the old MAGA chaos: tariffs waved like magic wands, certainty treated as weakness, and economic policy reduced to a series of impulsive taunts thrown at U.S. allies and rivals alike. | | ---|---|--- The result has been a manufacturing sector retreat--contracting for ten straight months, a sustained decline that no amount of Trump shrieking and podium pounding can wish away. As Barron's reports, The Institute for Supply Management's (ISM) manufacturing index fell to 47.9 from November's 48.2 reading, the lowest of 2025 despite modest improvements in employment | | ---|---|--- The consistent decline since Trump 2.0 took office is particularly noteworthy when you consider Old man Orange sold us his tariffs in 2025 as medicine. Trump said the tariffs would discipline foreign competitors, resurrect factories, and restore American industrial dominance. In practice, the Trump tariffs have functioned more like a recurring fever: raising costs for manufacturers, snarling supply chains, and making long-term planning an exercise in guesswork. | | ---|---|--- When steel, aluminum, and components grow more expensive overnight--and might grow more expensive again tomorrow--investment does not flourish. It hides. Manufacturing thrives on characteristics that sound boring: stability, clarity, and predictability. In other words, manufacturers need to have confidence that the rules will not change before the next quarterly report. Trump offers none of this. Instead, Trump delivers uncertainty as spectacle, a governing style that treats unpredictability as a strength. The MAGA crowd may roar. But economists wince and U.S. factories quietly scale back. | | ---|---|--- There is a special irony in watching Trump align himself with American manufacturing workers while presiding over policies that undermine the very industries Trump claims to champion. Protectionism, according to Trump, is a kind of muscular patriotism. In reality, protectionism is a tax. It is paid by producers, passed on to consumers, and absorbed by workers when factory orders diminish and shifts are cut. It is nationalism for people who do not read balance sheets, including the MAGA nutjobs. | | ---|---|--- Meanwhile, the comparison to the previous administration lingers like an inconvenient footnote Trump desperately keeps trying to erase. Manufacturing did not boom under Biden because he insulted trading partners or slapped tariffs on impulse. Manufacturing grew to record levels during the Biden administration because companies believed the future would resemble the present closely enough to justify investment. Confidence, not bravado, did the heavy lifting. | | ---|---|--- These days, Trump claims that America's economy under Biden was "dead." And in a suspension of reality and common sense that only Trump could imagine, Trump says we are the hottest economic nation in the world. When confronted with actual reality and statistics from the administration's own government agencies, Trump's defenders insist the manufacturing and industrial pain is temporary. Trump economic advisor Kevin Hassett and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent maintain that the ongoing contractions are a necessary sacrifice on the altar of future industrial greatness. | | ---|---|--- This is the same argument made by every economic quack throughout the industrial age: endure the harm now and trust the miracle will arrive later. Manufacturers, being practical and rationale people, are not buying it. So what we are left with in the U.S. under Trump is a familiar pattern: loud promises, thin results, and an insistence that basic facts are wrong, rather than the policy. Manufacturing is not declining because it lacks patriotism. It is declining because it lacks a U.S. President who understands that modern industry is built on cooperation, predictability, and trust--not haphazard tariffs. | | ---|---|--- If American manufacturing is going to recover, it will not be rescued by slogans or by a deranged President Trump who mistakes chaos for strength. U.S. manufacturing will recover once White House policy is grounded in economic sense rather than applause lines. Until then, Trump's vision of industrial revival remains a noisy farce, highlighted by bluster, and collapsing under basic arithmetic. The only thing Trump is growing in U.S. manufacturing, like so much else, is decline. | | ---|---|--- Invite your friends and earn rewards If you enjoy Blue Amp Media, share it with your friends and earn rewards when they subscribe. | | | | | | | (C) 2026 Cliff Schecter PO Box 8384, Cincinnati, OH 45208 ? ?

name: Ro Khanna
subject: How I passed ‘impossible’ bills in Congress
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ro for Congress
body:
https://rokhanna.com [https://rokhanna.com]
Peter,
Beltway insiders love to say that Congress can’t accomplish anything.
But this week, The Guardian published an in-depth profile on how I’ve been getting real results in Congress—passing legislation that seemed impossible by building coalitions that defy conventional Washington wisdom. It's a must-read.
READ: ‘I believe this is going to be a reckoning’: Ro Khanna, the man behind the Epstein files act, on building bipartisan wins. [https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/dec/28/ro-khanna-epstein-files-act-bipartisan]
https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/dec/28/ro-khanna-epstein-files-act-bipartisan [https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/dec/28/ro-khanna-epstein-files-act-bipartisan]
The story looks at some of the fights people said were impossible to win:
* The Epstein Files Transparency Act that forced sealed documents into public view;
* The historic Yemen War Powers Resolution , helping end U.S. support for a devastating, endless war; and
* The Chips and Science Act that's bringing semiconductor manufacturing back to American soil.
Here’s the lesson I’ve learned: real common ground doesn’t just happen in the center.
It happens at the edges where people on the left and right are fed up with endless wars, rigged systems, and a political class that never seems to face consequences.
As I put it in the article: "Good jobs at home, no dumb wars overseas, accountability for elites."
The article digs into how this kind of bipartisanship actually works: mastering the rules of Congress, building real trust across the aisle, and grounding every fight in moral convictions people genuinely share—not just splitting the difference for show.
You can read the full piece here [https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/dec/28/ro-khanna-epstein-files-act-bipartisan] . And after you do, text me at (650) 999-9610 and tell me:
What’s the fight Congress should take on next?
Thank you for your activism and all that you do.
In solidarity,
Ro
Wait! One more thing–
Ro doesn't accept a cent from lobbyists or PACs, meaning our work is completely funded by grassroots supporters like you. By donating just $3 today, you’re helping us create a country that works for everyone, not just those at the top. That means a renewed investment in American manufacturing, Medicare for All, student debt relief, and a green future.
If you want to support our people-powered campaign as we continue to fight for progress in Washington, please consider making a donation of $3 or more today. Thank you!

name: Team Stevens
subject: 5 years ago today
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Haley Stevens for Senate
body:
A day we'll never forget

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://act.haleyformi.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?&t=1&akid=1856%2E49239%2ENnfENR)
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11387?t=2&akid=1856%2E49239%2ENnfENR)
---
| | **LIVE POLL RE: JAN. 6**
---
**Weigh in TODAY: Do you support Donald Trump's pardons for Jan. 6 insurrectionists?**
---
| | | [YES](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11376?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=3&akid=1856%2E49239%2ENnfENR)
---
| [NO](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11376?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=4&akid=1856%2E49239%2ENnfENR)
---
null, five years ago today, a violent mob stormed the Capitol in an attempt to overturn the 2020 election results in Donald Trump's favor.
Police officers were attacked. Capitol staff and members of Congress hid, fearing for their lives. Hundreds of people were injured. People lost their lives.
Haley was there. She remembers. And she knows who's at fault.
[](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11376?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=5&akid=1856%2E49239%2ENnfENR)
And yet, Donald Trump issued a**sweeping pardon of everyone involved in the Jan. 6 insurrection.**
This is a gross injustice and a MASSIVE abuse of presidential power, null.

It is an insult to every Capitol police officer, staff member, and member of Congress who was there that day, as well as every American – because it was OUR democracy that was under attack.

And it is yet another reminder that whether it's election denial, attacks on our freedom to vote, or trying to rig maps in his favor, **Donald Trump will stop at nothing to undermine the will of voters and cling to power.**
**That is why, as we remember the lives lost, the injuries, and the grave threat our country faced five years ago, we are asking supporters in Michigan and across the country to condemn President Trump's actions.**
[**Please respond to our live poll before it closes at midnight.****TELL US NOW: Do you support Trump's pardons for Jan. 6 insurrectionists?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11376?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=6&akid=1856%2E49239%2ENnfENR)
| | | [YES](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11376?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=7&akid=1856%2E49239%2ENnfENR)
---
| [NO](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11376?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=8&akid=1856%2E49239%2ENnfENR)
---
Team Haley
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11377?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=9&akid=1856%2E49239%2ENnfENR) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/11387?t=10&akid=1856%2E49239%2ENnfENR)

You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley

name: Roy Cooper
subject: It seems that RFK Jr. is *trying* to get our kids sick
body:
This anti-vax ideology is going to harm American children and families.
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2365?source=&t=1&akid=304%2E124072%2EI-_Fzu)
Peter, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. — the unqualified, anti-science Secretary of Health and Human Services who last year fired our nation’s expert vaccine panel and appointed anti-vaccine conspiracy theorists in their place — announced his plan to overhaul the childhood vaccine schedule. Gone are the recommendations for vaccines for rotavirus, influenza, meningitis, hepatitis A, hepatitis B, and **even RSV — the nation’s leading cause of infant hospitalization.** **There is no reason that American children need to get sick from diseases that, in the year 2026, we have the ability to prevent.** Past generations of parents would have given anything to be able to protect their children from these illnesses. I can only imagine how they would be horrified at our rejection of the miracle of vaccines now. **[Our government’s duty is to protect the American people. RFK Jr.’s dangerous ideology is going to endanger their health and lives instead. I’m asking you to stand with me and speak out. Call on the federal government to protect vaccine access and encourage vaccination.](https://act.roycooper.com/sign/em_RC_PET_RFKJr_010626/?source=em_RC_PET_RFKJr_010626&t=7&akid=304%2E124072%2EI-_Fzu)** RFK’s justification for this overhaul is that he thinks our vaccination schedule should align with peer countries. _Peter, it already does._ The vaccinations American children receive were nearly identical to those given in Canada, the United Kingdom, Australia, and Germany. Meanwhile, the United States has an infant mortality rate consistently higher than our peer nations and a medical system that prices too many families out and fails rural communities altogether. And that was before Republicans made health care even _less_ accessible for millions. **All the more reason to make sure we prevent diseases when it’s in our power to do so.** Peter, experts agree and evidence has proven (over and over and over) that vaccines are safe and effective. Our children don’t need to get sick with rotavirus or risk death from RSV. Vaccines can protect them. Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and the Trump Administration would rather take those vaccines away. **[I am asking you to sign my petition today because this cannot be allowed to stand. Add your name to demand protections for vaccine access.](https://act.roycooper.com/sign/em_RC_PET_RFKJr_010626/?source=em_RC_PET_RFKJr_010626&t=8&akid=304%2E124072%2EI-_Fzu)** Thank you for your support, Roy Cooper [](https://act.roycooper.com/sign/em_RC_PET_RFKJr_010626/?source=em_RC_PET_RFKJr_010626&t=9&akid=304%2E124072%2EI-_Fzu)
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=2&akid=304%2E124072%2EI-_Fzu)** | [DONATE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=3&akid=304%2E124072%2EI-_Fzu)
---

[SHOP OUR MERCH STORE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2133?t=5&akid=304%2E124072%2EI-_Fzu)
[info@roycooper.com](mailto:info@roycooper.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.roycooper.com/go/825?t=6&akid=304%2E124072%2EI-_Fzu)

PO Box 1190
Raleigh, NC 27602

The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. name: Mark Kelly
subject: They can threaten my rank and my pension — but they’re not going to shut me up.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
You swear an oath to the Constitution when you join the military — including the First Amendment right to free speech. We swore to defend the foundational principles of this country. I took that oath seriously 40 years ago, and I still do.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/122806052/593516651/-2000534896?amount=10&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2026.01.06_Mark-Kelly-Split_B1_F1_S1_C1_90OP-NC_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODg1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MGJlMzM5Mi01YmViLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JnZsrJ8CbreZ8g4ymxveTUnhBj-QZIj69bPrE7IpwGg=) Team:

Pete Hegseth decided he's going to censure me, and is threatening to demote me and reduce my pension.

I got shot at in service to this country. I missed holidays and birthdays. I commanded a space shuttle mission and launched to the International Space Station while my wife recovered from a gunshot wound to the head.

My rank and retirement are things that I earned through my service and sacrifice for this country. Generations of servicemembers have done the same. Pete Hegseth wants to send a message to every single one that he will come after them if they say something he doesn’t like.

You swear an oath to the Constitution when you join the military — including the First Amendment right to free speech. We swore to defend the foundational principles of this country. I took that oath seriously 40 years ago, and I still do.

Pete Hegseth can threaten to reduce my rank and my pension. But I am not going to let him intimidate me, or anyone else. He is not going to stop me from defending our democracy from a president who is hellbent on destroying it.

If they think this is going to shut me up, they’ve got another thing coming.
